scenarios.scenario: Your chairman gives a 45-minute motivational speech to the players the night before a match. Players text you saying they're bored.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell them to respect the chairman.

scenarios.scenario: Your senior defender challenges the assistant referee to a footrace after a disputed offside call.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off with the officials.
scenarios.rightOption: Apologise formally on his behalf.

scenarios.scenario: The kit launch video for the new season features your players but one senior player was left out. He's livid.
scenarios.rightOption: Explain it was a timing issue.

scenarios.scenario: A local school headteacher writes an open letter praising your youth outreach. Fans share it wildly.
scenarios.leftOption: Use the goodwill for a public community day.
scenarios.rightOption: Respond with a quiet thank-you and move on.

scenarios.scenario: Performance data shows your set-piece routines are statistically the worst in the division.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick with them and trust the players.

scenarios.scenario: Your player misses three consecutive matches claiming anxiety. Club staff believe it might be contract-related.
scenarios.rightOption: Support him unconditionally regardless.

scenarios.scenario: The club's app sends a push notification saying 'sell everyone and start again' due to a coding error.
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate the security breach.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker has a standoff with a seagull at a corner. The crowd loves it. Referee is losing patience.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh and let it play out.
scenarios.rightOption: Call him over and refocus him.

scenarios.scenario: Your most important winger demands a promise: if he scores a hat-trick he gets to choose the next away kit design.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse as unprofessional.

scenarios.scenario: Your physiotherapist suggests introducing mandatory naps before afternoon training. Data backs it up.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it as too unconventional.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain loses his armband during a match. Three players try to claim it. A five-minute argument follows.
scenarios.leftOption: Designate the next in the pre-match protocol.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the trio sort it out themselves.

scenarios.scenario: A youth player's parents threaten legal action over what they call an unfair assessment process.
scenarios.rightOption: Contest it transparently.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant manager becomes an overnight social media star after a touchline reaction goes viral. He has 3 million followers in a week.
scenarios.leftOption: Embrace his fame for the club.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to tone it down.

scenarios.scenario: The board instructs you to loan your best defender to an overseas club the board has a commercial interest in.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse and risk the backlash.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores and immediately confronts a banner in the stands that mocked him last season.
scenarios.leftOption: Defuse the situation on the pitch.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the emotion run its natural course.

scenarios.scenario: A top agent calls to say his agency will blacklist your club if you don't sign one of his players immediately.
scenarios.leftOption: Call his bluff and reject the ultimatum.
scenarios.rightOption: Sign a fringe player from the agency to buy peace.

scenarios.scenario: Your number two is offered the head coaching job at a mid-table club. He asks your advice.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell him honestly it's a good move.
scenarios.rightOption: Try to convince him to stay.

scenarios.scenario: A high-profile transfer falls through on deadline day at 11:58 PM. The replacement you signed in panic is woeful.
scenarios.rightOption: Admit the mistake publicly.

scenarios.scenario: An indoor drone racing league offers to use your stadium for filming. It involves 40 drones at high speed.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline due to safety concerns.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper tweets that he would have saved the penalty that ended your cup run. He wasn't selected.
scenarios.leftOption: Drop him from the next squad.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a direct private conversation.

scenarios.scenario: Your two oldest players form a club within the squad called 'The Vintage Room.' Young players feel excluded.
scenarios.rightOption: Let them have their bonding.

scenarios.scenario: A local rival's fan forum posts a leaked version of your weekly fitness report. Accuracy is high.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a public statement calling it fabricated.

scenarios.scenario: Your new foreign signing insists on communicating via emoji only for the first month as a language barrier workaround.
scenarios.rightOption: Hire a translator on day one.

scenarios.scenario: Your centre back scores an overhead kick. He immediately retires on the pitch announcing: 'It's downhill from here'.
scenarios.leftOption: Try to talk him out of it in real time.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept his dramatic retirement and call for a substitute.

scenarios.scenario: The board schedules a press conference announcing your new contract extension but nobody told you the details.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand details before stepping in front of cameras.

scenarios.scenario: Your midfielder suffers a broken finger but insists on playing with a custom cast shaped like a pointing hand.
scenarios.rightOption: Require standard medical equipment.

scenarios.scenario: A club director promises fans a trophy in 18 months without consulting you. They print it on billboards.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as a rallying point for the squad.

scenarios.scenario: Your player's image is used in a rival team's kit launch ad without permission. He's thrilled. You're not.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the player enjoy his accidental celebrity.

scenarios.scenario: The board bans any mention of the word 'pressure' in official communications. Players find it absurd.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it as ridiculous.

scenarios.scenario: A footballer donates half his match-day win bonus to a charity without announcing it. A journalist finds out.
scenarios.leftOption: Publicise the act with his permission.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep it private as he apparently intended.

scenarios.scenario: Your first-choice striker breaks down crying after a goal and refuses to explain why. He wants a minute.
scenarios.leftOption: Give him the moment on the pitch.
scenarios.rightOption: Substitute him out of caution.

scenarios.scenario: Your club is offered an exhibition match against a team of world-famous musicians. Players are buzzing.
scenarios.leftOption: Agree and make it a media event.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep focus on league form.

scenarios.scenario: A player refuses to play in a specific shade of blue saying it gives him nightmares. The kit is already ordered.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to get over it.

scenarios.scenario: Your defensive midfielder gets a yellow card for excessive arguing and hasn't touched the ball yet.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave him on and manage the risk.

scenarios.scenario: Board members gift a signed stadium brick to season ticket holders. They forgot to consult the groundskeeper who owns the bricks.
scenarios.rightOption: Blame it on miscommunication.

scenarios.scenario: A player refuses to train on the artificial pitch claiming it's 'spiritually wrong'.
scenarios.rightOption: Make him train regardless.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's training is moved next to a busy road after stadium construction. A player is distracted by traffic and pulls a muscle.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it and adapt.

scenarios.scenario: A doping control officer arrives at training on the wrong day. Players are mid-session and unprepared.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask them to return on the scheduled day.

scenarios.scenario: Your club is shortlisted for a prestigious community award but the ceremony clashes with a relegated rival's relegation confirmation.
scenarios.rightOption: Attend the rival's match.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker wins the Player of the Year award but says you deserve Manager of the Year in his acceptance speech. You didn't win.
scenarios.leftOption: Thank him publicly and move forward.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay silent to avoid seeming entitled.

scenarios.scenario: A leaked internal memo suggests you're targeting three specific transfers. All three clubs raise their asking prices overnight.
scenarios.rightOption: Cool all three pursuits and go elsewhere.

scenarios.scenario: A pre-match motivational video accidentally plays a blooper reel instead. Players are crying with laughter.
scenarios.leftOption: Let the mood lift the team.
scenarios.rightOption: Quickly switch it and restore focus.

scenarios.scenario: Your top scorer wears a branded bandana that isn't part of the official kit. Sponsors want action taken.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him and enforce the rule.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it slide as harmless.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores a penalty by walking the ball into the net. Technically legal. Tactically bizarre.
scenarios.rightOption: Make him practice proper penalty technique immediately.

scenarios.scenario: A foreign journalist reveals your dressing room has a motivational quote wall with twelve spelling errors.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it as trivial.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's energy supplier cuts power to the training ground over an unpaid bill during the morning session.
scenarios.rightOption: Call a squad meeting outdoors immediately.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker misses the team photo because he forgot the date. It's printed on his contract.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it slide this once.

scenarios.scenario: Two youth players are caught recreating your tactics in a video game and posting the results on YouTube. The analysis is brilliant.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask them to take the videos down.

scenarios.scenario: Your most vocal fan group presents you with a handmade trophy for five years at the club. You've been here four.
scenarios.leftOption: Graciously accept and quietly correct them later.
scenarios.rightOption: Correct them publicly and thank them anyway.

scenarios.scenario: A player announces he's going vegan and within two weeks his sprint speed increases by 4%. Teammates want to know more.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it as his personal choice.

scenarios.scenario: Your winger brings a custom goal-celebration flag onto the pitch. It reads 'I Told You So.' Teammates love it.
scenarios.rightOption: Confiscate the flag.

scenarios.scenario: A player asks to play in bare feet citing cultural tradition for key matches.
scenarios.rightOption: Enforce standard boot policy.

scenarios.scenario: The board agrees to a mid-season fan Q&A where managers answer live questions. You'll be grilled live.
scenarios.rightOption: Try to get out of it.

scenarios.scenario: A club financial audit reveals you've been signed to the wrong contract tier. You're owed £500K.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept a revised contract quietly.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker attempts a scissors kick from the centre circle in the 92nd minute while losing 1-0.
scenarios.rightOption: Substitute him immediately for decision-making.

scenarios.scenario: A player returns from compassionate leave early saying football saved him. He's visibly not ready.
scenarios.leftOption: Support him and ease him back.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust his professional judgement.

scenarios.scenario: Your backup midfielder scores twice as a substitute and demands the starting spot. Your starter is your best player.
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate them in the next match.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep faith with your starter.

scenarios.scenario: A famous sports psychologist publishes research saying your preferred 4-3-3 causes psychological fatigue. Media goes crazy.
scenarios.rightOption: Dismiss it publicly.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper tries an audacious Cruyff turn in his own penalty box. It leads to a goal.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend his bravery.

scenarios.scenario: A player claims the opposing goalkeeper used mind games in the tunnel before the match. Your player missed two sitters.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it as gamesmanship.

scenarios.scenario: An artificial intelligence system correctly predicts every result for three straight weeks. The board wants to rely on it.
scenarios.leftOption: Use it as a supplementary tool.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it as a gimmick.

scenarios.scenario: A club legend at a charity dinner publicly offers your job to another manager saying 'he'd do it better'.
scenarios.rightOption: Confront the legend privately.

scenarios.scenario: Your player wins an international award but the club wasn't informed or invited to the ceremony.
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly address the snub with organizers.

scenarios.scenario: Your star player is offered an ambassadorial role with the club's shirt sponsor that takes up two days a month.
scenarios.rightOption: Block it to protect focus.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores then gestures to his contract wrist tattoo while staring into the camera. Agents are watching.
scenarios.leftOption: Call him in for a private contract discussion.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the theatre and praise the goal.

scenarios.scenario: A player knocks on your office door at 11pm asking for advice about his marriage. You barely know him.
scenarios.rightOption: Refer him to club counseling.

scenarios.scenario: A fan sews a life-size replica of your tactical dry-erase board and sells it on Etsy. It's accurate.
scenarios.rightOption: Request it be taken down for tactical sensitivity.

scenarios.scenario: The stadium DJ plays your resignation speech from a previous club as a prank on match day.
scenarios.rightOption: Complain to stadium management.

scenarios.scenario: Your coach is caught on camera doing keepy-uppies with a stray ball during a tense match. He's getting better.
scenarios.rightOption: Post it to the club's TikTok account.

scenarios.scenario: Board members vote to make your post-match press conferences shorter to help evening TV schedules. Journalists revolt.
scenarios.rightOption: Push back for media relations.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker wears a cap backwards during a press conference. The board's communications team is horrified.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to present more formally.

scenarios.scenario: A player completes his hat-trick then immediately FaceTimes his mum on the pitch.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him for the delay.

scenarios.scenario: The club's AI-powered scouting tool recommends buying a player from a club that's about to be dissolved. Price is rock bottom.
scenarios.leftOption: Rush the signing before the collapse.
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate why the club is failing first.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores 30 goals but his expected goals metric says he should have scored 40. Fans call him wasteful.
scenarios.rightOption: Use the data to motivate him.

scenarios.scenario: Your midfielder is late back from the international break due to a passport error. He's your most important player.
scenarios.leftOption: Delay team selection until he arrives.
scenarios.rightOption: Name the squad without him.

scenarios.scenario: A player reveals he's been tracking his own sleep data and challenges your training schedule with peer-reviewed science.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick with your methods.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant manager tries a motivational dance in the pre-match warm-up to hype the crowd. Players cringe visibly.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh and let him continue.

scenarios.scenario: A fan's homemade drone delivery of pizza to your bench during a match lands perfectly. The media loves it.
scenarios.leftOption: Tweet a thank-you and eat a slice.
scenarios.rightOption: Report the safety hazard to officials.

scenarios.scenario: Your top league scorer is also leading the league in yellow cards. Fifth yellow means a suspension next match.
scenarios.rightOption: Rest him to avoid suspension.

scenarios.scenario: The board demands a strategic plan for the next ten years. You rarely plan beyond the next match.
scenarios.leftOption: Write a creative but flexible document.
scenarios.rightOption: Push back and explain short-term thinking wins trophies.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker collapses celebrating a goal by sliding on his knees into a wooden advertising board.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh at the misfortune.

scenarios.scenario: A sponsor wants your club to wear a digital LED kit that changes colour mid-match based on performance.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it as a distraction.

scenarios.scenario: A referee's assistant confiscates your tactical clipboard saying it's a prohibited coaching aid mid-match.
scenarios.rightOption: Comply without fuss.

scenarios.scenario: The internet believes your star player and your assistant manager are the same person. The photos are uncanny.
scenarios.leftOption: Pose together to settle the debate.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the conspiracy theory.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores and immediately falls to his knees thanking the sky for 45 seconds. The ref adds injury time.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to celebrate faster.

scenarios.scenario: A club director publicly questions your loyalty after you attend a conference about global football management.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the comment.

scenarios.scenario: Your defender stops a certain goal by catching the ball with both hands. He said he forgot it wasn't basketball.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him for the handball.

scenarios.scenario: Performance software flags that your halftime team talks are making players 8% more tired in the second half.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the data.

scenarios.scenario: A player wants a piano in the changing room to play Beethoven before matches as motivation.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline as impractical.

scenarios.scenario: Your defender completes a 20-metre sprint in 2.3 seconds. Scientists say that's physically impossible.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep it quiet to avoid testing.

scenarios.scenario: A player publishes a children's book about a goalkeeper who never gives up. It's loosely about your reserve keeper.
scenarios.leftOption: Celebrate it as a community moment.
scenarios.rightOption: Point out the obvious passive-aggressiveness.

scenarios.scenario: Your first team's youth graduates rate is the lowest in the division. Academy fans are furious.
scenarios.leftOption: Introduce a minimum minutes guarantee for youth graduates.
scenarios.rightOption: Prioritize winning above development.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant manager is offered a punditry column writing contract by a major newspaper.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to decline to protect tactical secrecy.

scenarios.scenario: A player arrives to the pre-match meal wearing a suit with the opposing team's colours. He swears it's an accident.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him for poor judgment.

scenarios.scenario: Your coach-to-player earpiece starts playing a rival manager's tactical briefing by mistake. You hear everything.
scenarios.rightOption: Listen and benefit from the intelligence.

scenarios.scenario: Your youngest player is offered his own reality TV show mid-season.
scenarios.rightOption: Block it completely.

scenarios.scenario: A stadium monitor glitch shows your team's losing record during a live match. Fans react angrily in real time.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it as a glitch.

scenarios.scenario: Your two best midfielders both want to wear the number 10 shirt next season.
scenarios.leftOption: Give it to the better performer.
scenarios.rightOption: Introduce a shared honour system.

scenarios.scenario: Your squad refuses to do a traditional post-season awards trip saying they'd rather have rest.
scenarios.rightOption: Insist on the team bonding.

scenarios.scenario: A fitness tracker study shows that your assistant manager runs more during matches than most of your players.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep it as internal motivation.

scenarios.scenario: The board approves three transfer targets without consulting you. Two of them are in your system already.
scenarios.rightOption: Assert your authority and reject them.

scenarios.scenario: A player brings a lucky charm rabbit to every match. The rabbit escapes during a cup semi-final.
scenarios.rightOption: Call a brief time out to retrieve it.

scenarios.scenario: A famous fashion designer offers to redesign your training kit for free. It looks remarkable but nothing like the club colours.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline out of brand loyalty.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker celebrates a goal by producing a pair of sunglasses from his sock. Sponsor of the glasses is a rival kit brand.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him for the unsanctioned promotion.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go—it was genuinely stylish.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain attempts to cancel training via email while you are delivering the session in person.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a private word about chain of command.

scenarios.scenario: A boardroom whistleblower leaks that the club planned to sack you after a cup win. You just won the cup.
scenarios.leftOption: Demand a public statement of confidence.
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly renegotiate your contract from a position of strength.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker demands the ball be painted gold for him personally during shooting practice.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind him the league uses regulation balls.

scenarios.scenario: Three of your staff quit simultaneously citing poor communication from the board. It leaks to the press.
scenarios.leftOption: Hold a full staff crisis meeting.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a brief holding statement and carry on.

scenarios.scenario: A youth player scores a wonder goal in front of ten scouts. His agent immediately hikes his valuation by 400%.
scenarios.leftOption: Lock him down before the market inflates further.
scenarios.rightOption: Take the increased fee and sell.

scenarios.scenario: The league proposes removing relegation for five years to grow commercial interest. Your owners support it.
scenarios.rightOption: Oppose it as bad for competitive integrity.

scenarios.scenario: Your right back hasn't produced an assist in 40 games. He's in great physical shape but tactically ineffective.
scenarios.leftOption: Drop him to rotate in a more creative option.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep faith—his defensive work is elite.

scenarios.scenario: A player claims a ghost touched his leg causing him to miss a sitter. He's serious and files a welfare concern.
scenarios.rightOption: Address the miss tactically and move on.

scenarios.scenario: Your kit manufacturer releases your away kit early without approval. It divides opinion instantly—fans either love or hate it.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the kit and call it bold.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand it be recalled and repriced.

scenarios.scenario: Your midfielder goes AWOL to a meditation retreat for four days. He returns calmer and plays his best match of the season.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him but praise the performance.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as a template for squad wellness.

scenarios.scenario: An injury crisis leaves you with only 13 fit players for a crucial league match.
scenarios.leftOption: Ask the FA for a postponement.
scenarios.rightOption: Play youth players and wing it.

scenarios.scenario: The board insists you name a player who returned from a four-month ban as captain to signal a clean slate.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse—captaincy must be earned.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores every week in training but never in matches. Data says he's your best finisher statistically.
scenarios.leftOption: Play him anyway against the evidence.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop him and trust match-level output.

scenarios.scenario: A group of investors want to launch a fan token linked to your results. Every loss costs token holders money.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it as gambling adjacent.

scenarios.scenario: The match ball deflates mid-match and play continues for six minutes before officials notice.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh it off.

scenarios.scenario: Your physio recommends a complete ban on smartphone use for 12 hours before a match. Players revolt.
scenarios.rightOption: Compromise on a 6-hour ban.

scenarios.scenario: A club legend texts you personally asking for a coaching role on your staff. You have no vacancy.
scenarios.leftOption: Create a new role to bring him in.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely decline and preserve your structure.

scenarios.scenario: Your club reaches a cup final but the hotel books the wrong team for your rooms. You sleep in a rival hotel.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand compensation.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper dyes his hair to match the opposition's kit colour as a psychological tactic. It works for one match.
scenarios.rightOption: Enforce standard appearance requirements.

scenarios.scenario: Your defender loses his cool and argues with a corner flag for 30 seconds after a foul isn't given.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave him on and compose him via radio.

scenarios.scenario: Three fans send handwritten tactical advice weekly. One of them has better ideas than your coaching staff.
scenarios.leftOption: Credit the idea anonymously and use it.
scenarios.rightOption: Bin the letters.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain wants halftime talks to be replaced by silent group meditation. Four players are already converted.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick to standard halftime structure.

scenarios.scenario: The club installs a motivational quote by the chairman on the dressing room wall. Players find it ridiculous.
scenarios.rightOption: Remove it and send a polite note.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker publishes a self-help book mid-season claiming football gave him all the answers to life.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to wait until the season ends.

scenarios.scenario: A player tests positive for a banned supplement. He genuinely didn't know it was in his protein powder.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the ban and move on.

scenarios.scenario: The league sends a notice that your away fixture has moved cities due to stadium works. It's 200 miles further.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept and adjust the travel plan.

scenarios.scenario: Your fullback wins an award for the longest throw-in in league history. He wants a ceremonial re-enactment at the stadium.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline as unnecessary.

scenarios.scenario: Board members send 'constructive' half-time tactical diagrams via WhatsApp to your number two. He shows you during the talk.
scenarios.rightOption: Consider their input.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores with a backheel from outside the box. He then winks at the camera for 15 full seconds.
scenarios.rightOption: Celebrate with the squad and edit the wink into the highlights.

scenarios.scenario: A local charity gifts a rescue donkey to the club as a mascot. The donkey is very popular. It costs £200 a week to keep.
scenarios.rightOption: Rehome the donkey kindly.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper posts his own save percentage analysis online and it's wrong. He rates himself 94%. He's at 67%.
scenarios.leftOption: Send him the correct data privately.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the internet correct him.

scenarios.scenario: A player asks to wear a wellness wristband in club colours and publish the data publicly. Board isn't sure.
scenarios.leftOption: Support the transparency and player welfare angle.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline for medical privacy reasons.

scenarios.scenario: A photographer catches you asleep on the bench during a pre-season friendly. It makes the front page.
scenarios.rightOption: Complain to the photographer.

scenarios.scenario: Your squad votes unanimously to donate a match fee to a local hospital. One senior player abstains publicly.
scenarios.rightOption: Address the abstention at the next team meeting.

scenarios.scenario: A local rival drops to League Two while you're in the Premier League. Fans expect a generous response.
scenarios.leftOption: Offer a friendly and share revenue.
scenarios.rightOption: Focus on your own business.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's kit clashes with an opponent's third kit. Neither club brought the correct change strip.
scenarios.rightOption: Delay the match until a solution is sourced.

scenarios.scenario: Your star midfielder is photographed at a rival's pre-match fan zone the night before you face them. He says he was lost.
scenarios.leftOption: Start him and address it after.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop him until further notice.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands his image be removed from all club merchandise citing personal copyright. He's your top seller.
scenarios.rightOption: Comply and take the financial hit.

scenarios.scenario: The pitch invader from last season's famous win is now requesting a job at the club. Fans adore him.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely decline.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's trophy cabinet locks itself and the key is lost ahead of a museum event. A reporter makes it symbolic.
scenarios.rightOption: Drill the lock before the press arrives.

scenarios.scenario: A player completes a full match with a microphone pack clipped to his shorts for a documentary. It falls off and trips a rival player during a sprint.
scenarios.rightOption: Claim the rival was not injured and play on.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain delivers a rousing pre-match speech and nobody claps. The silence is deafening. He looks at you.
scenarios.leftOption: Applaud loudly and follow up with your own.
scenarios.rightOption: Deliver a tactical reinforcement and move past the silence.

scenarios.scenario: Your owner posts a match reaction video from the director's box in real time during a 4-0 defeat. Fans share it everywhere.
scenarios.rightOption: Embrace the transparency.

scenarios.scenario: A player achieves a personal milestone on the same day your rival achieves a club record. Media only covers the rival.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the media snub.

scenarios.scenario: Your club posts a transfer rumour denial at midnight that accidentally includes the player's actual fee. It's deleted in 40 seconds.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay silent and hope it blows over.

scenarios.scenario: Two former players your club released now play together for a top-four rival. Both score against you in a 3-1 defeat.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend your original decision.

scenarios.scenario: Your centre forward claps the opposition fans sarcastically after a disputed penalty. They love it. Your fans are nervous.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to focus on the game.

scenarios.scenario: Your new signing goes to the wrong stadium on debut day. His GPS took him to an ice rink of the same postcode.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him for the error.

scenarios.scenario: An anonymous club staff member writes a negative Glassdoor review of working for you. It gets 2,000 upvotes.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it as anonymous.

scenarios.scenario: Your club receives an offer for your worst performer. The fee is shockingly high. It looks like a favour to someone.
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate before agreeing.

scenarios.scenario: Your player wins league goal of the month but uses the interview to criticise the training facilities.
scenarios.leftOption: Praise the goal, address the facilities.
scenarios.rightOption: Focus only on the goal.

scenarios.scenario: A player refuses to train with the squad after discovering his squad number is also a rival player's favourite jersey number.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to get over it.

scenarios.scenario: Your board hires an external motivational coach for the squad without telling you. He's terrible and players complain.
scenarios.rightOption: Work around him.

scenarios.scenario: A player who was released publicly wishes the club well. Media calls it passive-aggressive. His stats agree.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it.

scenarios.scenario: Your top scorer says he'll only renew if his release clause is set at £500K. His market value is £40M.
scenarios.rightOption: Negotiate a compromise.

scenarios.scenario: A fringe player runs a betting pool in the dressing room on the score of training games. Winnings are donated to charity.
scenarios.rightOption: Shut it down citing regulations.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands you apologize to him live in training for subbing him off mid-match last week.
scenarios.rightOption: Stand by the decision without apology.

scenarios.scenario: Your club refuses to provide a guard of honour to a newly crowned champion. The manager calls it disrespectful.
scenarios.rightOption: Back your club's stance.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker accidentally wins a local pie-eating contest during a community visit. It's filmed and goes viral.
scenarios.rightOption: Find it unprofessional.

scenarios.scenario: The board suggests playing a full competitive match in virtual reality to attract a tech sponsor. FIFA disapproves.
scenarios.rightOption: Firmly reject the non-footballing distraction.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain loses the pre-match coin toss every single time. He wants to use a different coin.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him use his lucky coin.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him it doesn't matter.

scenarios.scenario: Your most reliable midfielder has started 42 consecutive matches. Your data says he needs rest. He says he's never felt better.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest him for his own good.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust his judgment and play him.

scenarios.scenario: A player insists on wearing two different coloured boots as his personal brand. One official sponsor clashes with the other.
scenarios.rightOption: Enforce uniform sponsorship.

scenarios.scenario: Your pre-season trip coincides with a solar eclipse. Half the squad refuse to train outdoors during the event.
scenarios.rightOption: Train regardless of astronomical events.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's social media team accidentally replies to a transfer rumour with 'yes' before deleting it.
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate the leak.

scenarios.scenario: A player publicly thanks his former club in a post-goal interview rather than his current one. He's been here three years.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it.

scenarios.scenario: Your entire coaching team tests positive for the flu the day before a semi-final.
scenarios.rightOption: Take charge yourself.

scenarios.scenario: Your late finish means a title rival wins the league in your stadium while you're still in the tunnel. Cameras catch you.
scenarios.rightOption: Avoid the handshake and walk off.

scenarios.scenario: Your defender scores an own goal then attempts to award himself a star-man performance because of his passion.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a serious word.

scenarios.scenario: A player gets fined by the FA for aggressive gesturing. You said the same thing on the touchline and weren't penalised.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the FA's ruling without comment.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands to bring his life coach to the pre-match warm-up. The life coach also wants to motivate the squad.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse the outside influence.

scenarios.scenario: The board approves a flash-mob performance by a dance troupe on the pitch before a huge home game. Players hate it.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it proceed.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper scores a direct free kick from 70 yards in a dead rubber. He wants the free kick duties next season.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse firmly.

scenarios.scenario: A youth player is signed by a rival after his father—a club employee—leaks training performance data.
scenarios.leftOption: Pursue legal action against the father.
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly release the father and protect the boy's career.

scenarios.scenario: Your club is offered a sponsorship by a space tourism company. The contract includes a player flight to orbit.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline on safety grounds.

scenarios.scenario: A manager survey ranks you as the most 'unapproachable' in the division. Your own players voted.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it humbly in a team meeting.
scenarios.rightOption: Dismiss the survey as a media-driven instrument.

scenarios.scenario: Your players refuse to eat the pre-match meal because the chef used a different brand of pasta. Third match in a row.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell them to eat it anyway.

scenarios.scenario: A player pens an open letter to the board praising you on the eve of a contract renewal meeting. You didn't ask him to.
scenarios.rightOption: Find it awkward.

scenarios.scenario: Your stadium's PA system plays the wrong national anthem before an international friendly. Guests are furious.
scenarios.rightOption: Blame the technician.

scenarios.scenario: A player earns more in commercial deals than from football. His priorities are visibly divided.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it as the modern game.

scenarios.scenario: Your club badge is redesigned by AI and goes viral. Half the fans love it. The other half are inconsolable.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick with the traditional one.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker wins both PFA and FWA Player of the Year. His agent immediately demands 300% wage increase.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse and risk losing him.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker has gone 8 games without a goal and blames the service from midfield.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand more from your midfield in training.

scenarios.scenario: A senior player arrives to training wearing a rival club's training kit as a 'joke'.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off and move on.
scenarios.rightOption: Send him home to change and fine him.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain demands he be allowed to take all set pieces despite poor delivery stats.
scenarios.rightOption: Assign set pieces based on training data.

scenarios.scenario: A player refuses to shake hands with an opponent after a contentious tackle.
scenarios.rightOption: Publicly apologize on the club's behalf.

scenarios.scenario: Your record signing has played 90 minutes in every game and demands a rest.
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate him for the next match.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him he's too important to rest.

scenarios.scenario: Two players competing for the same position refuse to room together on away trips.
scenarios.rightOption: Force them to room together to resolve it.

scenarios.scenario: A winger demands to play centrally or he'll request a transfer.
scenarios.leftOption: Try him in the new role.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse and remind him of his contract.

scenarios.scenario: Your veteran keeper is furious about not being considered for the captaincy.
scenarios.rightOption: Explain your selection criteria calmly.

scenarios.scenario: A player goes to the media claiming your training sessions are 'unprofessional'.
scenarios.leftOption: Call it lies and defend your staff.
scenarios.rightOption: Invite journalists to observe a session.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker celebrates a goal by pointing at the bench and mouthing 'that's for you'.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a private conversation about professionalism.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands the right to approve his own transfer rumours in the press.
scenarios.rightOption: Shut down the request immediately.

scenarios.scenario: Your backup striker gives an interview saying he deserves to start every game.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as motivation in the next selection talk.

scenarios.scenario: A player's wife publicly criticizes the club on a podcast.
scenarios.leftOption: Ignore it as a private matter.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask the player to address it with her.

scenarios.scenario: Your youth sensation demands adult wages after one breakthrough season.
scenarios.leftOption: Meet his demands to keep him.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick to the youth wage structure.

scenarios.scenario: A player posts a countdown timer on Instagram without explaining what it's counting to.
scenarios.leftOption: Ask him to take it down.
scenarios.rightOption: Embrace the mystery and let the hype build.

scenarios.scenario: Your top scorer requests a custom squad number mid-season for 'personal reasons'.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow the change as a goodwill gesture.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — squad numbers are set at season start.

scenarios.scenario: A player who was dropped leaks that the team is 'divided' to a tabloid.
scenarios.rightOption: Confront the player one-on-one and transfer list him.

scenarios.scenario: Your fullback has been caught arguing with fans outside the stadium after a loss.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him and apologize to fans.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend him — fans had no right to approach him.

scenarios.scenario: A player's agent demands he plays every cup game to boost his sell-on value.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse agent involvement in team selection.

scenarios.scenario: Two players got into a heated argument over a card game on the team bus.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the squad resolve it among themselves.

scenarios.scenario: The chairman announces a stadium name change sponsorship deal without consulting you.
scenarios.rightOption: Express disapproval in the next board meeting.

scenarios.scenario: The board wants to release three senior players to reduce the wage bill mid-season.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept the cuts to keep the board happy.
scenarios.rightOption: Fight to keep your key players.

scenarios.scenario: Your chairman is openly attending rival managers' press conferences as 'research'.
scenarios.rightOption: Request a formal meeting to discuss your position.

scenarios.scenario: The board demands you sell your best midfielder to a direct promotion rival.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the fee and find a replacement.

scenarios.scenario: Ownership wants to build luxury boxes over the away fans section.
scenarios.rightOption: Oppose it to protect supporter culture.

scenarios.scenario: The board announces a surprise pre-season tour to Asia with only 3 days notice.
scenarios.rightOption: Push back and request proper preparation time.

scenarios.scenario: Your chairman makes a handshake deal with a player's agent without telling you.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind the board that recruitment is your domain.

scenarios.scenario: The board votes to introduce VAR-style reviews for substitution decisions.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it as undermining managerial authority.

scenarios.scenario: A board member is spotted having dinner with a top agent linked to your replacement.
scenarios.rightOption: Say nothing and let your results speak.

scenarios.scenario: The club agrees a naming rights deal — the training ground will be called 'CryptoBase'.
scenarios.rightOption: Express concern about brand alignment.

scenarios.scenario: The chairman publicly disagrees with your formation choice after a defeat.
scenarios.rightOption: Stand firm and defend your tactical approach.

scenarios.scenario: The board wants to install cameras in the dressing room for a documentary series.
scenarios.leftOption: Agree — great for the club's profile.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it — the dressing room must remain private.

scenarios.scenario: Ownership expects you to hold a weekly video call with investors.
scenarios.rightOption: Delegate it to the club's PR director.

scenarios.scenario: The board wants to use your image in gambling advertisements.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse on personal principle.

scenarios.scenario: The club's new investor demands you play a 3-5-2 to suit the players he wants to buy.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore the request — tactics are your domain.

scenarios.scenario: A rival club submits a last-minute bid for your academy gem on deadline day.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — the fee is too good.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject all bids regardless of fee.

scenarios.scenario: Your new signing refuses to wear the squad number assigned to him.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him pick his preferred number.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to accept or return to his former club.

scenarios.scenario: A player you sold last season wants to return at double his old wages.
scenarios.leftOption: Bring him back at the inflated fee.
scenarios.rightOption: Look elsewhere — the wages don't fit your structure.

scenarios.scenario: Your transfer target fails a medical with a previously undisclosed injury.
scenarios.leftOption: Pull out of the deal entirely.
scenarios.rightOption: Complete the deal at a reduced fee.

scenarios.scenario: An agent approaches you directly with a player your scouts haven't vetted.
scenarios.rightOption: Follow your scouting process before deciding.

scenarios.scenario: A free agent superstar contacts you directly wanting to sign, but his wages are extreme.
scenarios.leftOption: Sign him for the PR and quality boost.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely decline — wages would destabilize the squad.

scenarios.scenario: Two of your players are caught negotiating a joint move to another club together.
scenarios.rightOption: Sit them down individually and resolve it.

scenarios.scenario: A player on loan from a rival club is outperforming your own squad members. The recall clause is triggered.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept the recall and find cover.
scenarios.rightOption: Try to negotiate an extension to the loan.

scenarios.scenario: The club receives a bid from a relegated club for your key player — he wants to go.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him leave — he's made his bed.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject and convince him to stay.

scenarios.scenario: Your transfer budget is cut by 40% two days before the window closes.
scenarios.leftOption: Make do with free agents and loans.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand an emergency board meeting.

scenarios.scenario: A group of players hires a private chef to override the club's nutrition plan.
scenarios.leftOption: Let them — results are what matter.
scenarios.rightOption: Reinforce the club's nutrition policy firmly.

scenarios.scenario: Your first-choice striker pulls a hamstring in training the day before a cup final.
scenarios.leftOption: Recall a loan player as emergency cover.
scenarios.rightOption: Promote the youth striker and back him.

scenarios.scenario: Players are secretly timing the manager's tactical talks and betting on length.
scenarios.leftOption: Shorten your talks to keep focus.
scenarios.rightOption: Confront the culture of disrespect.

scenarios.scenario: Your sports scientist recommends players train at 5am to optimize recovery windows.
scenarios.leftOption: Trial the early sessions for a month.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it — morale is more important than marginal gains.

scenarios.scenario: A player is caught using a banned supplement not on the registered list.
scenarios.leftOption: Report it immediately to the authorities.
scenarios.rightOption: Handle it quietly — investigate internally first.

scenarios.scenario: The club's training pitch is flooded and unplayable for a week.
scenarios.rightOption: Cancel training and give players gym time.

scenarios.scenario: A player's personal trainer contradicts your fitness coach's methods daily.
scenarios.leftOption: Ban personal trainers from the facility.
scenarios.rightOption: Find a compromise with both coaches.

scenarios.scenario: Two coaches disagree publicly during a drill session in front of the whole squad.
scenarios.leftOption: Take control and stop the session.
scenarios.rightOption: Let them resolve it — healthy debate is fine.

scenarios.scenario: Players are consistently arriving two minutes late to meetings. No one has said anything.
scenarios.leftOption: Set a new strict punctuality policy.
scenarios.rightOption: Address it humourously to reset the culture.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant suggests reducing ball-work and focusing purely on fitness for two weeks.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it — players need match sharpness.

scenarios.scenario: A player uses a training session to audition for an AI sports data company filming nearby.
scenarios.rightOption: Stop the filming and focus on training.

scenarios.scenario: Three players request mental health days on the same week before a big match.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to each player individually first.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper coach and fitness coach are feuding and refusing to work together.
scenarios.rightOption: Replace one of them immediately.

scenarios.scenario: Players who aren't selected keep leaving training early. No one has enforced it.
scenarios.leftOption: Introduce mandatory presence for all players.
scenarios.rightOption: Allow it — unused players need rest too.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker keeps faking contact in training drills to practice diving.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as a simulation tool for match situations.

scenarios.scenario: You're 3-0 up at halftime. Players look relaxed and start showboating.
scenarios.leftOption: Warn them and keep the shape.
scenarios.rightOption: Encourage them — enjoy the moment.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition keeps fouling your playmaker and the referee won't act.
scenarios.leftOption: Instruct him to go wider and avoid contact.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to stand his ground.

scenarios.scenario: You're 1-0 down with 20 minutes left against a team parked with 10 defenders.
scenarios.rightOption: Be patient — the goal will come.

scenarios.scenario: Your left winger is terrorizing the opposition. The right winger looks jealous and stops tracking back.
scenarios.leftOption: Substitute the right winger for a disciplined player.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a word with him at the next break in play.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition goalkeeper is wasting time from the first minute.
scenarios.leftOption: Instruct players to pressure him constantly.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay composed and trust the referee.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores then immediately limps. Do you substitute him on a hat-trick?
scenarios.leftOption: Replace him immediately — player welfare first.
scenarios.rightOption: Give him five minutes to see how it goes.

scenarios.scenario: An opposition player is clearly feigning injury every time you attack.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell your players to keep the ball moving and ignore it.
scenarios.rightOption: Lodge a formal protest with the referee.

scenarios.scenario: You're winning 1-0 in a must-win game. Your captain demands you push forward for a second.
scenarios.rightOption: Hold the shape and protect the lead.

scenarios.scenario: Your midfielder has just received his 5th yellow card and will be suspended for the next game.
scenarios.leftOption: Substitute him now to preserve energy.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him on — you need him today.

scenarios.scenario: The pitch markings are faded and causing confusion among your defenders.
scenarios.leftOption: Halt play and request the groundskeeper repaints lines.
scenarios.rightOption: Adapt and communicate more vocally.

scenarios.scenario: A laser is being pointed at your goalkeeper from the stands.
scenarios.leftOption: Request the match be temporarily suspended.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell your keeper to switch sides with the backup.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker is disputing every offside call and disrupting your team's rhythm.
scenarios.leftOption: Calm him down at the next break.
scenarios.rightOption: Let him channel the frustration into intensity.

scenarios.scenario: You've used all 3 subs and a key player goes down with cramp in the 75th minute.
scenarios.rightOption: Push him to play through for the last 15 minutes.

scenarios.scenario: Your team scores an outrageous long-range goal but VAR checks it for a foul in the build-up.
scenarios.rightOption: Furiously protest the length of the check.

scenarios.scenario: You're playing against a high press team. Your keeper is struggling with the ball at his feet.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell him to go long and bypass the press.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep playing out — trust the process.

scenarios.scenario: Halftime. You're drawing 0-0. The crowd is booing and your players look nervous.
scenarios.leftOption: Change formation and be more adventurous.
scenarios.rightOption: Back the team — tell them they're doing fine.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker scores but removes his shirt before reaching the stands. Second yellow card.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept it — passion comes with the territory.
scenarios.rightOption: Publicly criticize the moment of madness.

scenarios.scenario: The floodlights go out with 10 minutes to play and you're losing 1-0.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the delay and prepare for the restart.

scenarios.scenario: Your defensive midfielder is performing brilliantly but was booked for diving. He's furious.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell him to focus and forget it.
scenarios.rightOption: Back him completely and blame the referee.

scenarios.scenario: Your team wins a controversial penalty in the 90th minute. Fans of both sides are in uproar.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the decision and keep composure.
scenarios.rightOption: Admit it may have been soft but back your player.

scenarios.scenario: You're away from home and the travel schedule left players exhausted. The team looks flat at kickoff.
scenarios.leftOption: Make an early tactical change to spark energy.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick with the plan and trust fitness levels.

scenarios.scenario: An early red card leaves you with 10 men. The crowd is panicking.
scenarios.leftOption: Park the bus and play for a draw.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep playing your game — don't let fear dictate.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker refuses to press from the front when defending. The team is losing shape.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept his limitations and adjust the system.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition manager is screaming instructions directly at your players.
scenarios.leftOption: File a formal complaint with the fourth official.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it and let your players stay focused.

scenarios.scenario: Your team has just scored an 89th minute equalizer. Players sprint to celebrate in the away end.
scenarios.rightOption: Refocus them — there's still a minute left.

scenarios.scenario: A deep fake video of you making controversial tactical promises goes viral.
scenarios.rightOption: Make a humorous video clarifying the truth.

scenarios.scenario: Your star player goes live on Instagram during a team meeting without realizing.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep going — it's authentic content.

scenarios.scenario: A fan creates a song about your touchline antics that hits 10 million views.
scenarios.leftOption: Engage with it — react on social media.
scenarios.rightOption: Avoid acknowledging it — stay professional.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant's pre-match whiteboard photo leaks your tactical plans before kickoff.
scenarios.leftOption: Scramble to change the game plan.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it and trust your players to adapt.

scenarios.scenario: A player films teammates getting a tactical rollicking and posts it as a 'motivational' clip.
scenarios.leftOption: Praise him for sharing the culture.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him — dressing room is private.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's TikTok account posts a player's bloopers reel without his consent.
scenarios.rightOption: Remove the video and apologize to the player.

scenarios.scenario: A player's controversial Twitter/X thread about team spirit goes viral overnight.
scenarios.leftOption: Hold a media blackout for 48 hours.
scenarios.rightOption: Address it head-on in your next press conference.

scenarios.scenario: A photo of your training tactics board goes viral and is ridiculed by pundits.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off — it was taken out of context.
scenarios.rightOption: Address the misunderstanding in a press briefing.

scenarios.scenario: A local blogger publishes a piece calling your transfers 'the worst in club history'.
scenarios.leftOption: Respond with your transfer success stats.
scenarios.rightOption: Rise above it — don't give it oxygen.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's official account accidentally posts a private team GC message publicly.
scenarios.rightOption: Own the mistake and make light of it.

scenarios.scenario: Fan groups are organizing a march demanding the board invest in transfers.
scenarios.rightOption: Remain neutral to protect the board relationship.

scenarios.scenario: A player confronts a fan who has been heckling him for three home games in a row.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a formal apology to the fan.

scenarios.scenario: Fans vote in an online poll that your tactics are 'embarrassing'. 80% agreement.
scenarios.rightOption: Dismiss it as uninformed opinion.

scenarios.scenario: The supporter trust requests a meeting with you to discuss youth policy.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — external input doesn't belong in operations.

scenarios.scenario: Home fans start chanting a rival player's name during a bad performance.
scenarios.leftOption: Acknowledge it drives you to improve.
scenarios.rightOption: Call it disrespectful to your squad.

scenarios.scenario: A fans' banner is unfurled calling for the chairman's resignation — mid-match.
scenarios.rightOption: Approach the fourth official to have it removed.

scenarios.scenario: Supporters write an open letter praising your appointment and asking for one more season.
scenarios.rightOption: Let results speak — words are easy.

scenarios.scenario: Away fans are consistently outsinging the home support. Players notice.
scenarios.leftOption: Challenge your players to feed off it.
scenarios.rightOption: Meet with the fan engagement team to fix it.

scenarios.scenario: A season ticket holder writes a viral letter claiming you 'ruined their matchday experience'.
scenarios.leftOption: Invite them for a one-on-one conversation.
scenarios.rightOption: Address it with a general apology to all fans.

scenarios.scenario: Fan groups are boycotting merchandise over a club rebrand they weren't consulted on.
scenarios.leftOption: Support the fans — push back on the rebrand.
scenarios.rightOption: Back the board's commercial decision.

scenarios.scenario: A pundit who was once your teammate publicly calls your tactical approach 'naive'.
scenarios.leftOption: Challenge him to come and see training.
scenarios.rightOption: Rise above it — his opinion doesn't matter.

scenarios.scenario: A journalist claims to have insider information about a dressing room rift.
scenarios.leftOption: Deny it and threaten legal action.
scenarios.rightOption: Invite the journalist to speak to players directly.

scenarios.scenario: You're asked about your future at the club in a post-match interview after a defeat.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse to answer — it's not the time.

scenarios.scenario: A broadcaster asks to do a fly-on-the-wall access documentary.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — media access should stay controlled.

scenarios.scenario: Your press conference is interrupted by a protesting fan who gets past security.
scenarios.rightOption: Walk out and reschedule.

scenarios.scenario: A national newspaper gives your last ten signings a collective D- transfer rating.
scenarios.leftOption: Respond with detailed stats to counter the narrative.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — papers need clicks.

scenarios.scenario: You're accused of time-wasting in a press conference by an opposition manager.
scenarios.leftOption: Apologize and commit to better conduct.
scenarios.rightOption: Fire back with your own accusations.

scenarios.scenario: A podcast with 500k listeners claims to have 'exclusive' audio of your halftime team talk.
scenarios.rightOption: Find the leak and deal with them quietly.

scenarios.scenario: You slip up and use a banned word during a live press conference.
scenarios.rightOption: Claim it was taken out of context.

scenarios.scenario: A reporter ambushes you outside the stadium with questions about a player's personal life.
scenarios.leftOption: Decline to comment and keep walking.
scenarios.rightOption: Stop and set the record straight.

scenarios.scenario: Three of your key players return from international duty with minor muscle strains.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest them for the next game.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the medical team and make a decision on the day.

scenarios.scenario: A national team manager asks you to release a player mid-season for a non-competitive friendly.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — he's mid-injury recovery.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain returns from international duty claiming the national manager 'destroyed his confidence'.
scenarios.leftOption: Give him a confidence-boosting run of games.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to leave international issues at the door.

scenarios.scenario: A player scores a hat-trick for his national team but returns exhausted 48 hours before your match.
scenarios.leftOption: Start him anyway — he's in form.
scenarios.rightOption: Rest him and bring in a fresh option.

scenarios.scenario: An international break falls just as your team hits a winning run of five games.
scenarios.leftOption: Use it to drill new patterns into the team.
scenarios.rightOption: Give players complete rest — protect the momentum.

scenarios.scenario: Your 17-year-old academy star is wanted by a top European club's academy.
scenarios.leftOption: Block the move — he belongs here.
scenarios.rightOption: Let him decide — his development matters most.

scenarios.scenario: An academy player breaks the dress code wearing flashy designer gear to training.
scenarios.leftOption: Make an example and enforce the code.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a quiet word — don't kill his personality.

scenarios.scenario: Your youth coach wants to promote six academy players to the first-team bench.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the decision — give youth a chance.
scenarios.rightOption: Insist on experience in the squad.

scenarios.scenario: A parent of an academy player confronts you at the training ground about their son's lack of game time.
scenarios.leftOption: Invite them for a proper sit-down meeting.
scenarios.rightOption: Refer them to the academy manager.

scenarios.scenario: Two academy prospects ask to leave on loan to get first-team experience.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep one and let one go.

scenarios.scenario: Five first-team players are injured simultaneously. The media calls it a 'crisis'.
scenarios.leftOption: Promote youth players and reshape the team.
scenarios.rightOption: Spend emergency funds on short-term loan signings.

scenarios.scenario: A player is secretly playing through a serious knee injury to avoid letting the team down.
scenarios.leftOption: Stand him down immediately regardless of his protests.
scenarios.rightOption: Respect his choice but increase monitoring.

scenarios.scenario: Your physio is concerned a player may be faking injury to avoid selection.
scenarios.leftOption: Call a specialist in for a second opinion.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop the player as a message to the squad.

scenarios.scenario: A player tears his ACL in training and is out for 9 months. He publicly blames the training intensity.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept some responsibility and review intensity.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend your methods — injuries happen in football.

scenarios.scenario: Your backup goalkeeper injures himself warming up. You have no recognized third choice.
scenarios.leftOption: Start an outfield player in goal.
scenarios.rightOption: Rush a free agent emergency signing.

scenarios.scenario: The referee books you on the touchline for the third time this season.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept it and ask a coach to continue your instructions.
scenarios.rightOption: Publicly question the referee's consistency post-match.

scenarios.scenario: You're told a VAR decision that went against you was an 'acknowledged error'.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept the apology and move on.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand the league review the officiating standard.

scenarios.scenario: The referee in your next match has given more red cards against your club than any other.
scenarios.leftOption: Warn your players not to give him any reason.
scenarios.rightOption: Request the appointment be reviewed.

scenarios.scenario: A linesman waves offside when replays clearly show the goal was legal.
scenarios.leftOption: Protest immediately — goal needed to stand.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the decision and refocus.

scenarios.scenario: Post-match data shows your team was incorrectly denied three clear penalties over two games.
scenarios.leftOption: Present the data publicly and demand answers.
scenarios.rightOption: Share the data privately with the board.

scenarios.scenario: You receive a job offer from a Champions League club midway through the season.
scenarios.leftOption: Reject it — your club comes first.
scenarios.rightOption: Meet with them privately before deciding.

scenarios.scenario: The media reports you've been spotted looking at houses in another city.
scenarios.leftOption: Deny it publicly and recommit to the club.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay silent and let results do the talking.

scenarios.scenario: You're offered a lucrative TV punditry deal to be fulfilled during the international break.
scenarios.leftOption: Decline — full focus on the job.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept — it's a good platform for the club.

scenarios.scenario: Your family is photographed at a rival club's match and it sparks speculation.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it honestly — family watches football.
scenarios.rightOption: Avoid commenting — it's a private matter.

scenarios.scenario: You're voted 'Manager of the Month' but your team loses in the same week.
scenarios.leftOption: Celebrate the award and use it as motivation.
scenarios.rightOption: Downplay the award — only the result matters.

scenarios.scenario: A betting company offers you 10x your salary for one sponsored post.
scenarios.rightOption: Consult the board before deciding.

scenarios.scenario: A ghost-written autobiography attributed to you is published without your approval.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh it off and write a real one yourself.

scenarios.scenario: You storm off the pitch at halftime and the cameras catch your body language.
scenarios.leftOption: Go back out and show composure.
scenarios.rightOption: Own the passion — the team needed to see it.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition manager shakes your hand before the game but ignores you at full time.
scenarios.leftOption: Call him out professionally in the press.
scenarios.rightOption: Say nothing — results spoke for themselves.

scenarios.scenario: A journalist reveals your pre-match meal routine and it becomes a national joke.
scenarios.rightOption: Change your routine and address the leak.

scenarios.scenario: Players are split on whether to take a knee before matches. Argument breaks out.
scenarios.leftOption: Mandate a unified stance as a club.
scenarios.rightOption: Let players make individual choices.

scenarios.scenario: The dressing room playlist has become a war zone between different music factions.
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate the DJ rights weekly by agreement.
scenarios.rightOption: Ban personal music — introduce club-chosen sessions.

scenarios.scenario: Several players are forming cliques based on nationality. Training atmosphere suffers.
scenarios.leftOption: Hold a mandatory team-building away day.
scenarios.rightOption: Address it directly with the captain.

scenarios.scenario: A senior player is caught intimidating younger squad members about social media posts.
scenarios.rightOption: Handle it through the captain confidentially.

scenarios.scenario: Players run a secret sweepstake on the next tactical formation you'll use.
scenarios.rightOption: End it immediately — undermines tactical preparation.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain privately tells you three players are considering refusing contract renewals together.
scenarios.rightOption: Call a full squad meeting to clear the air.

scenarios.scenario: Players are openly laughing at tactical instructions from your assistant coach.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend your staff and address the disrespect.

scenarios.scenario: The squad organizes a karting day without inviting the coaching staff.
scenarios.rightOption: Let them enjoy their bond — space matters.

scenarios.scenario: A player brings his dog to training and half the squad is obsessed with it.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind everyone this is a professional environment.

scenarios.scenario: The squad demands a full day off after a poor run of results to 'reset mentally'.
scenarios.leftOption: Grant the day — mental health matters.
scenarios.rightOption: Insist on training — discipline is needed right now.

scenarios.scenario: A snow storm is forecast the day before a vital home match.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the groundskeeping team to prepare the pitch.

scenarios.scenario: Three matches get rescheduled in the same week due to a rivals' cup run.
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate aggressively and trust the depth.
scenarios.rightOption: Play your strongest lineup in every game.

scenarios.scenario: Extreme heat makes the pitch unplayable during a pre-season tour match.
scenarios.rightOption: Play it — squad needs the minutes.

scenarios.scenario: Fog rolls in mid-match making it impossible to see across the pitch.
scenarios.rightOption: Continue — your team is comfortable in chaos.

scenarios.scenario: The main shirt sponsor adds an embarrassing logo mid-season without player consultation.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the sponsor — it's a board decision.
scenarios.rightOption: Request an emergency meeting with the commercial team.

scenarios.scenario: A kit supplier sends the wrong shorts to the stadium on matchday.
scenarios.leftOption: Play in training shorts as a makeshift solution.
scenarios.rightOption: Delay kickoff while sourcing the correct kit.

scenarios.scenario: A local business approaches you to open a restaurant in your name.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — distractions must be minimized.

scenarios.scenario: The kit manufacturer wants players to wear an experimental new boot in a live match.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — comfort and familiarity come first.

scenarios.scenario: Your star forward demands a clause that lets him leave if you get sacked.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — no player dictates contract terms.

scenarios.scenario: A player earns more than your entire coaching staff combined. He knows it.
scenarios.leftOption: Address wage structure with the board.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — it's a board decision.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper blames a defender for every goal conceded — on the pitch, loudly.
scenarios.leftOption: Substitute the keeper for squad discipline.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to both players separately post-match.

scenarios.scenario: A forward insists on taking all throw-ins near the opposition box. It's slowing attacks.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him — he's a key man.
scenarios.rightOption: Correct the habit firmly in training.

scenarios.scenario: Your loan player's parent club instructs him not to risk himself in tackles.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept their terms to keep the loan.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject their interference and return him early.

scenarios.scenario: A player's tattoo of your club's badge causes controversy due to its design.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off as extreme loyalty.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to get it modified.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker celebrates a goal by sitting down on the pitch and refusing to move.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop him for the next game for showboating.

scenarios.scenario: A midfielder publishes a self-help book during the season calling football 'just a job'.
scenarios.leftOption: Back his right to express himself.
scenarios.rightOption: Ensure it hasn't damaged dressing room culture.

scenarios.scenario: Your player refuses to sub off when shown the board, walks slowly and argues.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to him post-match and keep it in-house.

scenarios.scenario: A player scores an own goal and then laughs at himself in the press conference.
scenarios.rightOption: Warn him that professionalism comes first.

scenarios.scenario: Your playmaker demands the ball every time regardless of the pressing situation.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept it — he makes things happen.
scenarios.rightOption: Coach him into reading the press better.

scenarios.scenario: A player skips the post-match cooldown every week to get to his car first.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go — results are more important.

scenarios.scenario: Your team's top scorer hasn't spoken to the press all season. Media are furious.
scenarios.leftOption: Encourage him to do one interview.
scenarios.rightOption: Protect his preference — football is the priority.

scenarios.scenario: A player wears noise-cancelling headphones during your pre-match team talk.
scenarios.leftOption: Remove them and address the disrespect.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it pass — he's a known introvert.

scenarios.scenario: Your defender marks the wrong player and concedes. He then blames the goalkeeper.
scenarios.leftOption: Make him apologize to the keeper publicly.
scenarios.rightOption: Address it privately in the debrief.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands to be the only one to speak to referees during matches.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject — captains handle referee communication.

scenarios.scenario: Your academy product tells the media he never wants to leave the club. Rivals offer £40m.
scenarios.leftOption: Reject the bid — honour his words.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept and reinvest in the squad.

scenarios.scenario: A player demands a personal chef be hired at the club's expense after reading about elite clubs.
scenarios.leftOption: Fund it as an investment in performance.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely decline — the canteen is sufficient.

scenarios.scenario: Your forward has started refusing to track back. He says 'that's not my job'.
scenarios.leftOption: Drop him to make a point.
scenarios.rightOption: Redesign the system to protect him.

scenarios.scenario: A player gets a portrait of himself tattooed on his own arm. Squad finds it hilarious.
scenarios.leftOption: Let the banter flow — good for morale.
scenarios.rightOption: Discourage excessive ego displays.

scenarios.scenario: The board tables a plan to move the club to a new city to grow the fanbase.
scenarios.rightOption: Remain neutral and let the board decide.

scenarios.scenario: A billionaire threatens to withdraw investment unless you're replaced.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the board's right to make the call.
scenarios.rightOption: Publicly challenge the threat.

scenarios.scenario: The board hires a Director of Football who overrules one of your signings.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept the veto — work within the structure.
scenarios.rightOption: Escalate to the chairman immediately.

scenarios.scenario: The board refuses to build a recovery pool despite mounting injuries.
scenarios.rightOption: Commission a private report on injury costs.

scenarios.scenario: A board member undermines you in a fan Q&A session live on YouTube.
scenarios.leftOption: Request a private meeting to resolve tension.
scenarios.rightOption: Respond publicly to correct the narrative.

scenarios.scenario: The board approves a kit design your players hate without your input.
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly relay player dissatisfaction to the chairman.

scenarios.scenario: New ownership wants to rebrand the club with a completely different badge.
scenarios.rightOption: Lead the opposition on behalf of fans.

scenarios.scenario: The board refuses compensation to a youth coach you want to promote.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept and find an internal solution.

scenarios.scenario: Your chairman wants to introduce a performance-related pay clause into your contract.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — you believe in your ability.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject — pressure shouldn't change your approach.

scenarios.scenario: The club announces a kit price increase of 30% the week after a bad run.
scenarios.rightOption: Support it — clubs need revenue.

scenarios.scenario: The board cuts the youth scouting budget by half after a poor financial quarter.
scenarios.rightOption: Fight for the budget — future of the club depends on it.

scenarios.scenario: The chairman wants to fast-track a major signing without a medical.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept given the circumstances and tight deadline.

scenarios.scenario: You've won three straight using a 4-3-3 but analysts say opponents have figured you out.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the system — don't fix what isn't broken.

scenarios.scenario: Your fullbacks are bombing forward but leaving you exposed. You're up 1-0.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell them to hold positions for the last 20 mins.
scenarios.rightOption: Let them keep going — attack is the best defense.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker is unmarked at the back post but never makes the run. He scores from elsewhere.
scenarios.rightOption: Drill the back post run regardless of his preference.

scenarios.scenario: Your press has been bypassed three times in a row. The system looks exposed.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the press — one adjustment will fix it.

scenarios.scenario: You're playing a team that sits very deep. Your wingers are useless against a low block.
scenarios.leftOption: Bring on your target man and go direct.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the wingers on and be patient.

scenarios.scenario: Your dominant pressing style is causing fatigue in a congested fixture schedule.
scenarios.leftOption: Take your foot off the press for a couple of matches.
scenarios.rightOption: Maintain the intensity — it's your identity.

scenarios.scenario: You've been playing three at the back but your centre-back is on a yellow card.
scenarios.leftOption: Revert to four at the back to protect him.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep three at the back — protect your structure.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker asks to drop deeper to link play but it leaves you with no striker.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him experiment — trust his vision.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him as an out-and-out striker.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition manager has successfully neutralized your preferred shape three times.
scenarios.leftOption: Surprise them with a completely new formation.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick to your principles — tweak minor details.

scenarios.scenario: Your team scores three goals from set pieces in one game. Fans want more set piece drills.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep balance — don't over-specialize.

scenarios.scenario: You switch to a high press away from home and concede three on the counter.
scenarios.leftOption: Abandon the press for away games.
scenarios.rightOption: Tweak the press rather than abandoning it.

scenarios.scenario: A 17-year-old has earned a start through sheer training performance. The press is watching.
scenarios.leftOption: Start him — reward the work rate.
scenarios.rightOption: Give him 30 minutes as a sub instead.

scenarios.scenario: Your strikers are pressing high but midfield isn't tracking — leaving gaps everywhere.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell midfield to join the press harder.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop striker line to close the gap.

scenarios.scenario: You want to debut a new counter-pressing system but players aren't drilled in it yet.
scenarios.leftOption: Trial it in the next match anyway.
