scenarios.rightOption: Wait until the international break to drill it properly.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper is having a nightmare. You have one sub left. Use it on him?
scenarios.rightOption: Save the sub — he'll find his way back.

scenarios.scenario: A sub comes on and immediately argues with a teammate about position.
scenarios.rightOption: Intervene from the touchline and resolve it.

scenarios.scenario: You make three subs at once and the team loses the plot — concedes immediately.
scenarios.leftOption: Hold your nerve and make no more changes.
scenarios.rightOption: Calm the team from the touchline urgently.

scenarios.scenario: Your intended sub refuses to warm up. He expected to start.
scenarios.rightOption: Use a different sub and deal with it post-match.

scenarios.scenario: You bring on a winger and he scores within 60 seconds of coming on.
scenarios.leftOption: Trust the sub to continue and press for another.
scenarios.rightOption: Protect the lead and revert to defensive shape.

scenarios.scenario: A player who's been dropped storms out of the ground after being benched again.
scenarios.leftOption: Confront him publicly and discipline him.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to him privately — he's clearly struggling.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain demands to know why he's being substituted at halftime while the camera rolls.
scenarios.leftOption: Answer honestly and calmly on camera.
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly tell him to keep it for the dressing room.

scenarios.scenario: A reporter calls your 4-4-2 'dinosaur football' at a packed press conference.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh and ask if dinosaurs won titles.
scenarios.rightOption: Explain your tactical rationale calmly.

scenarios.scenario: You're asked if you'd drop a player for attitude — and he's sitting next to you.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask the player to leave before answering.

scenarios.scenario: Your post-match interview is broadcast globally and you accidentally praise the wrong opponent.
scenarios.leftOption: Correct yourself and move on gracefully.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a formal clarification post-broadcast.

scenarios.scenario: You walk into a press conference to find the previous manager's chair still at the table.
scenarios.leftOption: Ignore it and get on with things.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask for it to be removed before you sit.

scenarios.scenario: A journalist reveals your personal phone number has been shared on a fan forum.
scenarios.leftOption: Alert the club's security team immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh it off — football is open enough.

scenarios.scenario: You're asked to give your prediction for the title race live on national TV.
scenarios.rightOption: Deflect — focus on your own game.

scenarios.scenario: A journalist quotes a player's agent claiming you've 'lost the dressing room'.
scenarios.leftOption: Deny it firmly and back your squad.
scenarios.rightOption: Invite the press into training to see for themselves.

scenarios.scenario: You go on a live rant about fixture congestion. It becomes a viral clip.
scenarios.leftOption: Double down — it needed to be said.
scenarios.rightOption: Walk back the comments in the next press conference.

scenarios.scenario: A foreign press outlet translates your interview incorrectly and creates a scandal.
scenarios.leftOption: Issue a correction through official channels.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — domestic audience is what matters.

scenarios.scenario: You're offered a weekly radio slot to discuss football. Management would love the exposure.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — good platform for the club.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — too many distractions already.

scenarios.scenario: A player's partner live-tweets your half-time team talk from the stands using second-hand info.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a ban on phones in the family section.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's social media team posts a meme mocking a rival club. It gets 500k likes.
scenarios.rightOption: Remove the post — rivalry should stay on the pitch.

scenarios.scenario: A player creates an anonymous football meme account that's going viral — yours included.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind him of his social media obligations.

scenarios.scenario: A former player goes on a podcast and rates every current squad member publicly.
scenarios.leftOption: Let it fuel the squad's motivation.
scenarios.rightOption: Block all squad access to the podcast.

scenarios.scenario: Your training video edited with viral music gets 2M views — but exposes your set pieces.
scenarios.leftOption: Leave it up — the morale boost is worth it.
scenarios.rightOption: Take it down and review content approval processes.

scenarios.scenario: A player's reaction video to a new signing goes viral for all the wrong reasons.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to the player privately about professionalism.

scenarios.scenario: The club's chatbot on the website starts giving incorrect transfer information.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it and correct errors manually.

scenarios.scenario: A player's 'goals compilation' montage on YouTube deliberately excludes three teammates.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the algorithm handle who notices.

scenarios.scenario: A deepfake of your voice is used in a betting ad. It's convincing and everywhere.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a clear denial and move on.

scenarios.scenario: Your club's Instagram story accidentally shows the tactics board before a Champions League match.
scenarios.leftOption: Delete it and hope it wasn't screenshotted.
scenarios.rightOption: Own the mistake with a joke post.

scenarios.scenario: Fans hang a 'Save Our Club' banner after rumours of a leveraged buyout.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay neutral and focus on football.

scenarios.scenario: Supporters call for a minute's applause for a club legend — during your team's warm-up.
scenarios.leftOption: Stop the warm-up and lead the applause.
scenarios.rightOption: Acknowledge it after the match.

scenarios.scenario: An away end is closed by the host club. Your fans are stranded with nowhere to sit.
scenarios.leftOption: Delay your pre-match preparations to help coordinate.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it to the club administrators.

scenarios.scenario: Fan groups demand the introduction of standing sections in the stadium.
scenarios.rightOption: Stay neutral — it's a board and safety decision.

scenarios.scenario: A group of ultras start choreographing TikTok dances in the stands instead of supporting.
scenarios.leftOption: Invite them to get behind the team instead.
scenarios.rightOption: Appreciate the creativity and engagement.

scenarios.scenario: Away supporters wave a banner insulting one of your players' personal life.
scenarios.leftOption: Make a formal complaint to the away club.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell your player to use it as fuel.

scenarios.scenario: The home end sings a rival manager's name to taunt you. It catches on.
scenarios.leftOption: Use it as motivation in your team talk.
scenarios.rightOption: Acknowledge it and promise a reaction.

scenarios.scenario: Fans organize a 'no noise' protest against ownership. The ground is silent.
scenarios.leftOption: Speak publicly in support of the protest.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask fans to get behind the team regardless.

scenarios.scenario: Supporters' trust buys a 1% stake in the club. They expect a board seat.
scenarios.rightOption: Remain professional but cautious.

scenarios.scenario: A fan streaks across the pitch during a pivotal match. Players burst out laughing.
scenarios.leftOption: Let the moment pass — refocus on the game.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask for stricter security measures post-match.

scenarios.scenario: Your physio recommends microchip tracking implants for players to monitor load. Half refuse.
scenarios.leftOption: Make it mandatory for the squad.
scenarios.rightOption: Make it optional and respect player choice.

scenarios.scenario: Three players get injured in the same warm-up drill. The session design is questioned.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend the methodology — injuries are random.

scenarios.scenario: A player returns early from injury against medical advice because 'the team needs him'.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow it — his mentality is incredible.
scenarios.rightOption: Stand him down — protect the long-term.

scenarios.scenario: An injury crisis leaves you playing a central midfielder at right back.
scenarios.leftOption: Back him in the unfamiliar role.
scenarios.rightOption: Play a youth player in position instead.

scenarios.scenario: The medical team says your best player needs a rest week. There's a cup final in 8 days.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest him for the week and trust the recovery.
scenarios.rightOption: Manage his training load but keep him involved.

scenarios.scenario: A player who just returned from injury asks to play 90 minutes in a low-stakes game.
scenarios.leftOption: Give him the confidence boost of 90 minutes.
scenarios.rightOption: Manage his minutes carefully — 60 max.

scenarios.scenario: Your star player is limping but gesturing he's fine from the pitch.
scenarios.leftOption: Trust him — he knows his body.
scenarios.rightOption: Substitute him immediately.

scenarios.scenario: New wearable tech flags a player as 'high risk of hamstring injury'. He feels perfectly fine.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust his feeling — the data isn't always right.

scenarios.scenario: Several players want to observe Ramadan fasting while maintaining a training schedule.
scenarios.leftOption: Adapt training times to accommodate them fully.
scenarios.rightOption: Maintain the schedule — individual arrangements are private.

scenarios.scenario: A new foreign signing is homesick and struggling to integrate. He's your biggest transfer.
scenarios.leftOption: Pair him with a senior player as a mentor.
scenarios.rightOption: Give him time — homesickness is natural.

scenarios.scenario: Players from different countries celebrate national holidays in incompatible ways. Scheduling clash.
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate training to honour each occasion.
scenarios.rightOption: Maintain one consistent schedule for all.

scenarios.scenario: A player refuses to participate in a club diversity training day claiming it's 'political'.
scenarios.leftOption: Make the session mandatory with consequences.
scenarios.rightOption: Have a personal conversation to understand his view.

scenarios.scenario: Your club is approached to host a Pride event on match day. Board is undecided.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the board make the call — stay professional.

scenarios.scenario: The squad's whatsapp group leaks to the press. It contains memes about the chairman.
scenarios.leftOption: Protect the players — it's private banter.
scenarios.rightOption: Address the leak and tighten phone policy.

scenarios.scenario: A player runs into the dressing room during training to place a bet on your match. Staff saw it.
scenarios.leftOption: Report it to the FA immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Handle it internally through the PFA.

scenarios.scenario: Senior players have started an unofficial 'fines jar' for late arrivals. Junior players are uncomfortable.
scenarios.rightOption: Shut it down — you run discipline here.

scenarios.scenario: A veteran player 'retires' from headers citing long-term health concerns. Games are approaching.
scenarios.leftOption: Support his choice and adjust tactics.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask for more time — have a medical team assess.

scenarios.scenario: Two players argue loudly about who deserves more followers on social media. Squad watches.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind them of their responsibilities immediately.

scenarios.scenario: A player cries in the changing room after being dropped. Other players don't know how to react.
scenarios.leftOption: Speak to him privately and involve the team.
scenarios.rightOption: Give him space and speak to him one-on-one.

scenarios.scenario: Your squad organizes a penalty shootout tournament at training. Keeper vs outfield.
scenarios.rightOption: End the session — time is limited.

scenarios.scenario: A player finds out he's on the lowest wage in the squad and confronts you directly.
scenarios.leftOption: Be honest and explain the wage structure.
scenarios.rightOption: Direct him to the club secretary for contract talks.

scenarios.scenario: Your squad has started ironically calling you by the name of your least favourite manager.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh — it shows they're comfortable with you.
scenarios.rightOption: Make clear it stops now.

scenarios.scenario: Players are sleep-deprived because of a team hotel noisy renovation mid-week.
scenarios.leftOption: Move the squad to a different hotel immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Get earplugs and make do with the situation.

scenarios.scenario: You're drawn against a lower league club in the cup. Media says it's a walkover.
scenarios.leftOption: Field the strongest lineup to avoid embarrassment.
scenarios.rightOption: Rest key players — the league is the priority.

scenarios.scenario: A cup replay has been scheduled during a crucial league run. The board wants you to win both.
scenarios.leftOption: Go all out in both competitions.
scenarios.rightOption: Prioritize the league — use the cup to blood youth.

scenarios.scenario: The club hasn't won a trophy in 20 years. A cup run has the city buzzing.
scenarios.leftOption: Make the cup your priority this season.
scenarios.rightOption: Don't get distracted — stay focused on the league.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper saves a penalty in a cup shootout and sprints to the opposition end to celebrate.
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him for provoking opposition fans.

scenarios.scenario: You're 2-0 up in a cup final with 10 minutes left. Your captain wants to entertain.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the structure — don't concede in a final.

scenarios.scenario: Five players are out of contract in 6 weeks. None have agreed renewals.
scenarios.leftOption: Open emergency talks with all five simultaneously.
scenarios.rightOption: Prioritize the two most important and let the rest go.

scenarios.scenario: Your best player signs a pre-contract with a rival club. Season still has 12 games to go.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him playing — you need him for the run-in.

scenarios.scenario: The board offers to extend your contract by one year. Your results merit three.
scenarios.rightOption: Negotiate firmly for the deal you deserve.

scenarios.scenario: A player accepts terms at a rival club at midnight on the last day of the season.
scenarios.leftOption: Wish him well and move on.
scenarios.rightOption: Confront him and make clear he's burned bridges.

scenarios.scenario: The board wants to exercise a release clause on a player you were planning to keep.
scenarios.leftOption: Let the business run its course.
scenarios.rightOption: Fight to keep him — he's central to your plans.

scenarios.scenario: A player rejecting your contract offer leaks the wage details to the press.
scenarios.rightOption: Call him out publicly for the breach of trust.

scenarios.scenario: A pre-season friendly finishes 6-0 and your tactical plans are broadcasted everywhere.
scenarios.leftOption: Brush it off — results don't matter pre-season.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand stricter filming restrictions at friendlies.

scenarios.scenario: A local non-league club asks you for a pre-season friendly. Your players expect a bigger name.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely decline and find a higher-quality opponent.

scenarios.scenario: Pre-season training reveals a player has let his fitness drop severely over the summer.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him and put him on an intensive plan.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to him privately to understand what happened.

scenarios.scenario: Pre-season results have been poor. Media calls it 'warning signs'. Squad is tight.
scenarios.leftOption: Ignore the noise — pre-season means nothing.
scenarios.rightOption: Use the performances to drive urgency in training.

scenarios.scenario: A fringe player runs a 5km personal best during pre-season fitness tests. Demands recognition.
scenarios.rightOption: Manage expectations — fitness is baseline, not exceptional.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant leaves his clipboard in the opposition dug-out at halftime.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off — retrieve it quietly.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as a teaching moment about preparation.

scenarios.scenario: A club legend crashes your press conference uninvited with advice for the team.
scenarios.rightOption: Politely but firmly redirect proceedings.

scenarios.scenario: An owl lands on the pitch during a night match and refuses to leave. Play is halted.
scenarios.leftOption: Embrace the surreal moment with the crowd.
scenarios.rightOption: Pressure officials to resolve it quickly.

scenarios.scenario: Your goal celebration is accidentally identical to a rival manager's signature move.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep it — maybe he copied you.

scenarios.scenario: A match ball boy retrieves the ball too slowly for your goalkeeper during a vital attack.
scenarios.leftOption: Complain formally to the fourth official.
scenarios.rightOption: Focus your team on the bigger picture.

scenarios.scenario: Local schools want to use your training pitch on weekends during the off-season.
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — the pitch needs rest and maintenance.

scenarios.scenario: A player scores from his own half by accident and the crowd goes wild.
scenarios.leftOption: Celebrate and make him the hero.
scenarios.rightOption: Privately laugh but stay professional on the touchline.

scenarios.scenario: The stadium announcer mispronounces your name at every home game. It's become a running joke.
scenarios.leftOption: Correct it once and move on.
scenarios.rightOption: Embrace it as part of the folklore.

scenarios.scenario: Your team sheet is submitted with an error — a player listed who isn't in the building.
scenarios.leftOption: Admit the clerical error and correct immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Try to substitute him out at the earliest opportunity.

scenarios.scenario: A sponsor offers to name the half-time whistle sound after their brand.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — free revenue for the club.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject — ridiculous commercialization of the game.

scenarios.scenario: Your left back has started nutmegging teammates in training just for applause.
scenarios.leftOption: Channel the flair — it builds confidence.
scenarios.rightOption: Shut it down before it creates resentment.

scenarios.scenario: A player you dropped scores a hat-trick for the reserve team. He gives a sarcastic interview.
scenarios.leftOption: Call him up immediately for first team.
scenarios.rightOption: Let him sweat it out — attitude matters.

scenarios.scenario: Your striker demands his shirt number is retired when he leaves. He's still at the club.
scenarios.leftOption: Dismiss it as a future decision.
scenarios.rightOption: Shut it down — no one is bigger than the club.

scenarios.scenario: A fringe player turns up to training in a sports car worth more than your annual wage.
scenarios.leftOption: Ignore it — personal wealth is irrelevant.
scenarios.rightOption: Use it as a motivational tool for hungry players.

scenarios.scenario: Your winger flicks the ball up and volleys it on target in a league match. It misses. He grins.
scenarios.leftOption: Love the ambition — great for the crowd.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to focus on the basics.

scenarios.scenario: A player releases a fashion collaboration mid-season that demands constant promotional events.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand all external work stops until the season ends.

scenarios.scenario: Your veteran striker cries during the national anthem and the cameras catch it.
scenarios.leftOption: Embrace the emotion — it fires the squad.
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to him about managing emotions pre-match.

scenarios.scenario: A player posts a cryptic '🚪' emoji after being dropped. Fans go wild with speculation.
scenarios.rightOption: Call him and ask him to delete it immediately.

scenarios.scenario: Your captain demands he reads the starting XI aloud to the team in the dressing room.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow it — he's earned the respect.
scenarios.rightOption: That's the manager's job — decline politely.

scenarios.scenario: A player scores then immediately calls his agent from the pitch on a hidden phone.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him for the breach of protocol.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh — he's a character and he scored.

scenarios.scenario: Analysts say your team has the worst chance conversion rate in the league despite dominating.
scenarios.leftOption: Bring in a specialist finishing coach.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the process — the goals will come.

scenarios.scenario: Your team plays brilliant football but sits 12th. The board wants pragmatism over beauty.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend your identity — the league table will come.

scenarios.scenario: Opposition scouts are filming your training session from outside the perimeter fence.
scenarios.leftOption: Call security and have them removed.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — all teams do this.

scenarios.scenario: Your game plan relies on a player who has slept 4 hours due to a newborn at home.
scenarios.rightOption: Give him a rest today regardless of the plan.

scenarios.scenario: You want to play an extremely high defensive line but your slowest defender is your best.
scenarios.leftOption: Trust the plan — speed of thought over legs.
scenarios.rightOption: Drop the high line — protect your weakness.

scenarios.scenario: A key player asks to understand the tactical reason behind every single decision you make.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind him his job is to execute, not to question.

scenarios.scenario: Your team has gone 8 games without a clean sheet. The defence blames the midfield.
scenarios.leftOption: Bring the team together for a defensive workshop.
scenarios.rightOption: Call out the defensive unit directly.

scenarios.scenario: Your 4-2-3-1 is statistically your worst formation by xG allowed. Analytics recommend 4-3-3.
scenarios.leftOption: Make the switch based on data.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust your coaching instinct over the data.

scenarios.scenario: The stadium PA system blares the wrong walkout music — it's the opposition's anthem.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off and build on the chaos.
scenarios.rightOption: Complain to stadium management immediately.

scenarios.scenario: The home end is only 40% full for a crucial match due to a public transport strike.
scenarios.leftOption: Acknowledge the fans who made it.
scenarios.rightOption: Demand the club provide transport alternatives.

scenarios.scenario: A former player returns as opposition and is applauded by your own fans throughout.
scenarios.rightOption: Remind your players the loyalty is to the badge.

scenarios.scenario: The tannoy announces the wrong score from another game mid-match, causing confusion.
scenarios.rightOption: Formally complain to the stadium operator.

scenarios.scenario: Your stadium's changing rooms have no hot water before a freezing winter match.
scenarios.leftOption: Demand the opposition's facilities be shared.
scenarios.rightOption: Adapt — warriors don't need warm showers.

scenarios.scenario: The stadium clock is wrong by 8 minutes. Players lose track of time in the second half.
scenarios.leftOption: Alert the fourth official and request correction.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — players should have inner focus.

scenarios.scenario: Rival fans set off a flare that lands on the pitch during your attack.
scenarios.leftOption: Stop play and ensure player safety.
scenarios.rightOption: Play on — stopping breaks your momentum.

scenarios.scenario: The opposition manager shouts offensive comments at your players from the dug-out.
scenarios.leftOption: Report it to the fourth official immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell your players to channel the anger into performance.

scenarios.scenario: It's the 93rd minute, 0-0, and you have one sub left. Do you risk it for the win?
scenarios.leftOption: Bring on an extra striker — go for glory.
scenarios.rightOption: Hold shape — the draw keeps the run going.

scenarios.scenario: You're winning but your striker is deliberately slowing the game with ball-juggling.
scenarios.leftOption: Tell him to keep it but play quicker.
scenarios.rightOption: Sub him — it's disrespectful to the opponent.

scenarios.scenario: Your team is clearly superior but keeps giving the ball away with over-elaborate play.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the quality — they'll find their rhythm.

scenarios.scenario: You win a corner in the 89th minute while losing. Go short or send it into the box?
scenarios.rightOption: Send it in and gamble on a header.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper wants to come up for every set piece in the last 10 minutes — you're losing.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow it — desperate times need desperate measures.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him in goal — don't abandon the structure.

scenarios.scenario: Both of your centre-backs are carrying minor knocks. Do you sub one off at halftime?
scenarios.leftOption: Sub one off — protect for the run-in.
scenarios.rightOption: Play on — they can manage the second half.

scenarios.scenario: An opposition player feigns injury to waste time and your player calls it out loudly.
scenarios.rightOption: Tell your player to stay out of it.

scenarios.scenario: You win a penalty in extra time of a cup match. Your regular taker is exhausted.
scenarios.rightOption: Choose a fresh sub who's shown confidence.

scenarios.scenario: Your club adopts an AI-powered team selection tool. It picks a lineup you disagree with.
scenarios.leftOption: Override the AI and pick your team.
scenarios.rightOption: Follow the recommendation and observe the outcome.

scenarios.scenario: Players are using VR headsets to study opposition movements. Half are sick from motion.
scenarios.leftOption: Pull the programme — player comfort first.
scenarios.rightOption: Push through — elite performance requires discomfort.

scenarios.scenario: A drone filming your training is grounded by a player who throws a ball at it.
scenarios.leftOption: Discipline the player for destroying property.
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh it off — the player was protecting the session.

scenarios.scenario: Data shows a starting player's performance drops 30% in the second half every game.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust his experience to manage his own levels.

scenarios.scenario: A sensor company wants to attach performance chips to your squad's training bibs.
scenarios.leftOption: Agree — elite clubs use this everywhere.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — players find it invasive.

scenarios.scenario: Live match analytics suggest a substitution your gut disagrees with. It's halftime.
scenarios.leftOption: Follow the data — it's probably right.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust your coaching instinct.

scenarios.scenario: Your team is denied three penalties in a match. The referee later retires.
scenarios.leftOption: Use it as fuel in the next game.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a formal request for explanation from the FA.

scenarios.scenario: A referee who previously sent off your manager is appointed for your next match.
scenarios.leftOption: Prepare your team to stay disciplined.
scenarios.rightOption: Request a formal review of the appointment.

scenarios.scenario: Your team scores from an indirect free kick that went direct. The referee counts it.
scenarios.leftOption: Say nothing — you'll take the goal.
scenarios.rightOption: Come clean to the referee immediately.

scenarios.scenario: The referee's assistant incorrectly flags your player offside from the kick-off.
scenarios.leftOption: Make a formal protest at half time.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it and move on — it's one call.

scenarios.scenario: A referee issues you a touchline ban mid-season for accumulated dissent.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept it and coordinate from the stands.
scenarios.rightOption: Appeal the ban immediately.

scenarios.scenario: An 18-year-old trains better than your £20m signing but lacks experience.
scenarios.leftOption: Start the youngster — form beats price tags.
scenarios.rightOption: Use him as an impact sub while protecting him.

scenarios.scenario: Your academy director resigns publicly citing lack of first-team pathways.
scenarios.leftOption: Issue a rebuttal with evidence of youth graduates.
scenarios.rightOption: Meet him and understand the root cause.

scenarios.scenario: Three academy players reject contract offers and leave for rivals.
scenarios.leftOption: Review the academy's offering with the board.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it — top talent will always have options.

scenarios.scenario: A 16-year-old prodigy is offered terms but insists on a release clause at a fraction of his value.
scenarios.leftOption: Reject the clause — it's too risky.
scenarios.rightOption: Sign him — the talent outweighs the risk.

scenarios.scenario: Your youth team wins a prestigious tournament. The first team players seem threatened.
scenarios.leftOption: Celebrate the youth team loudly and publicly.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the celebration internal — manage senior egos.

scenarios.scenario: You need a win from the last game to avoid relegation. Your best player is suspended.
scenarios.leftOption: Rebuild the team around what you have.
scenarios.rightOption: Play an emergency youth player in his role.

scenarios.scenario: You're one point off the top with two games left. Players want to know the bonus structure.
scenarios.rightOption: Focus on the football — money follows results.

scenarios.scenario: A draw in the final game secures a historic Champions League spot. Fans want a title challenge.
scenarios.rightOption: Publicly commit to going for the title next season.

scenarios.scenario: You've mathematically avoided relegation with four games left. Do you rotate?
scenarios.leftOption: Rotate heavily — give fringe players a run.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the best team playing — momentum matters.

scenarios.scenario: A goal celebration sees five players slide on their knees in the mud on a freezing pitch.
scenarios.rightOption: Quickly check them for injuries before it happens again.

scenarios.scenario: You're about to clinch the title but need your rivals to drop points. Players are watching their phones.
scenarios.leftOption: Encourage the watch party — it's fine.
scenarios.rightOption: Ban phones — focus on your own performance.

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant is overheard telling players you're 'difficult to work with'.
scenarios.leftOption: Confront him directly and offer a resolution.
scenarios.rightOption: Report it to the board and begin severance talks.

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeping coach has developed a close friendship with the first-choice keeper. Others feel left out.
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go — a strong relationship is beneficial.

scenarios.scenario: A member of your coaching staff wants to trial unconventional meditation before matches.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow it as optional for interested players.
scenarios.rightOption: Keep pre-match routines standard.

scenarios.scenario: Your set piece coach developed your most successful routine. A rival club is trying to poach him.
scenarios.leftOption: Fight to keep him with a contract upgrade.
scenarios.rightOption: Let him go — the methods are already embedded.

scenarios.scenario: A new analyst you hired is generating brilliant data but clashes with the senior coaching staff.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the analyst and demand integration.
scenarios.rightOption: Let the senior staff manage him out.

scenarios.scenario: Your fitness coach leaves at the end of the season. The team's fitness is currently excellent.
scenarios.rightOption: Go external — fresh ideas needed.

scenarios.scenario: A heat wave forces training to be moved to 6am. Senior players refuse the new schedule.
scenarios.leftOption: Enforce it — conditions leave no choice.
scenarios.rightOption: Find a compromise time that works for all.

scenarios.scenario: A violent thunderstorm stops play with your team winning 3-0. The match may be abandoned.
scenarios.leftOption: Lobby hard for the result to stand.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the decision — football follows safety rules.

scenarios.scenario: Heavy rain makes the ball stick in puddles. Your passing game is useless.
scenarios.leftOption: Switch to direct long balls immediately.
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the technique — quality players adapt.

scenarios.scenario: Extreme cold causes the electronic substitution board to malfunction.
scenarios.leftOption: Use hand signals and verbal communication.
scenarios.rightOption: Delay the sub until the board is fixed.

scenarios.scenario: Gale force winds completely negate your aerial game plan for a cup match.
scenarios.rightOption: Persist — your players are technically better.

scenarios.scenario: A documentary crew follows you for a week but captures a heated training session.
scenarios.rightOption: Approve it — authenticity builds connection.

scenarios.scenario: A national newspaper runs a poll and 68% of readers want you sacked.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it head on in your next conference.
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore polls — only results matter.

scenarios.scenario: You are the subject of a satirical sketch on prime time TV.
scenarios.leftOption: Engage with it on social media humorously.
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a formal complaint to the broadcaster.

scenarios.scenario: A radio presenter suggests your contract should have a minimum points clause.
scenarios.rightOption: Privately consider whether it aligns with your goals.

scenarios.scenario: Two journalists arrive at your press conference clearly hungover.
scenarios.leftOption: Let it go — they still have a job to do.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask the club's media team to address standards.

scenarios.scenario: A player's contract has a clause that doubles his wages if he starts 20 games. He's on 19.
scenarios.leftOption: Start him in the next match — he deserves it.
scenarios.rightOption: Bench him — protect the wage bill.

scenarios.scenario: The club can't afford a striker and the board suggests converting a midfielder.
scenarios.rightOption: Reject — buy a striker or go with youth.

scenarios.scenario: A player wants loyalty bonus written into his contract. The board refuses.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the board's position.

scenarios.scenario: Your wage budget is cut but a free agent superstar approaches you directly.
scenarios.leftOption: Stretch the budget and make it work.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick to the budget — discipline builds culture.

scenarios.scenario: A player's goal bonus has gone unpaid for three months due to an admin error.
scenarios.leftOption: Ensure it's paid today with an apology.
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it to the finance department to resolve.

scenarios.scenario: A player proposes to his partner on the pitch at full time after a win. Cameras everywhere.
scenarios.rightOption: Ask him to keep personal celebrations for after.

scenarios.scenario: You find an anonymous note in your dug-out mid-match. It reads: 'Switch to 4-3-3 now'.
scenarios.rightOption: Look into who left it after the match.

scenarios.scenario: A player earns more in one week than your entire youth coaching staff earn in a year.
scenarios.leftOption: Raise it with the board as a values issue.
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it — that's the modern game.

scenarios.scenario: Your opposite number refuses to shake your hand at kickoff, citing 'poor sportsmanship'.
scenarios.rightOption: Confront him on the touchline professionally.

scenarios.scenario: A streaker sprints through your technical area during a five-minute VAR check.
scenarios.leftOption: Use the moment to lighten the atmosphere.
scenarios.rightOption: Request tighter security from the stadium team.

scenarios.scenario: Your tactical briefing slides accidentally go to the wrong club via email.
scenarios.leftOption: Contact the recipients immediately and request deletion.
scenarios.rightOption: Change the game plan completely as a precaution.

scenarios.scenario: You score a wonder goal in a charity match and the crowd goes wild. Players are in awe.
scenarios.leftOption: Keep it humble — it's just for charity.
scenarios.rightOption: Milk every second — morale boost for the squad.

scenarios.scenario: A rival manager buys your autobiography and reads it before you meet them.
scenarios.rightOption: Make sure the next edition has wrong tactics in it.

scenarios.scenario: A club legend tweets criticism at your starting XI twenty minutes before kickoff.
scenarios.rightOption: Show your team the tweet as extra motivation.

scenarios.scenario: Your players are late to a charity gala due to traffic and arrive mid-speech.
scenarios.rightOption: Blame the travel team and make it official.

scenarios.scenario: Your star player's agent calls to say a top club has submitted a formal bid. The agent hints the player wants to explore it.
scenarios.leftOption: Reject outright, tell agent he's not for sale
scenarios.rightOption: Ask to speak to the player privately first

scenarios.scenario: An agent cold-calls offering a free agent striker who was recently released for disciplinary reasons — 'a changed man', he insists.
scenarios.leftOption: Sign him on a short-term deal and monitor
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — the risk isn't worth it

scenarios.scenario: Your captain's agent leaks to the press that he's unhappy with his contract. You find out before it becomes a headline.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it publicly — confirm talks are ongoing
scenarios.rightOption: Call the captain directly and keep it in-house

scenarios.scenario: A rival club tries to poach your assistant manager with a head coach role. He comes to you for advice.
scenarios.leftOption: Encourage him to go — it's his dream
scenarios.rightOption: Offer him a pay rise to stay

scenarios.scenario: Two agents are simultaneously pitching you on their clients — one a flashy winger, one a reliable utility player — for the same transfer budget.
scenarios.leftOption: Sign the flashy winger for marketing upside
scenarios.rightOption: Sign the utility player for tactical depth

scenarios.scenario: Your captain publicly criticises your substitution decisions after a loss. Other players are watching to see how you respond.
scenarios.leftOption: Defend your decision publicly — you won't be undermined
scenarios.rightOption: Meet him privately and hear his frustration out

scenarios.scenario: Your captain has been struggling physically. A younger player is clearly outperforming him but hasn't been given the armband.
scenarios.leftOption: Strip the armband and give it to the new leader
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the captain — loyalty matters for morale

scenarios.scenario: Three senior players approach you as a delegation, asking you to drop the team's newest signing who they say 'doesn't fit the culture'.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the new signing fully and dismiss the request
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate quietly — maybe there's something to it

scenarios.scenario: The supporters' trust threatens to stage a walkout in the 5th minute of your next home match if the board doesn't commit to a stadium investment.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the fans publicly — pressure the board alongside them
scenarios.rightOption: Ask fans to stand down — the timing is damaging

scenarios.scenario: Your ultras group is blamed for throwing a flare onto the pitch during a match, which gets your club a partial stadium ban.
scenarios.leftOption: Publicly condemn the action and cooperate with authorities
scenarios.rightOption: Defend the fans — the context was provoked

scenarios.scenario: Fans have started a viral campaign mocking your tactical setups with meme videos. Even your players are sharing them.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off publicly — show you have humour
scenarios.rightOption: Address the tactical criticism directly in a press conference

scenarios.scenario: You're 2-0 down at halftime. Your assistant suggests a bold 4-2-4 shape. Your gut says stick with 4-3-3 and adjust personnel.
scenarios.rightOption: Stick to 4-3-3 but shuffle the personnel

scenarios.scenario: You're 1-0 up at halftime in a must-win match. Your striker is on a yellow card and playing brilliantly.
scenarios.leftOption: Substitute him off — can't risk the red
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him on — you need his output

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper is making mistakes but says he's fine. Your backup goalie is ready. The match is tight at 0-0.
scenarios.leftOption: Sub the keeper — form matters more than pride
scenarios.rightOption: Trust your first-choice keeper to recover

scenarios.scenario: The referee has made two controversial calls against you. Your bench is furious and being warned. You're losing 1-0 with 20 minutes left.
scenarios.leftOption: Stay calm — don't give ref another excuse
scenarios.rightOption: Vent tactically — push your players to press aggressively

scenarios.scenario: With 10 minutes left in a 1-1 draw you need to win, you have one substitute left. A winger for extra pace or a midfielder to control possession?
scenarios.leftOption: Bring on the pacy winger to attack
scenarios.rightOption: Bring on the midfielder to dominate possession

scenarios.scenario: A fan captures your angry touchline reaction on video. It goes viral overnight with mixed reactions — some love the passion, others find it embarrassing.
scenarios.leftOption: Post a self-deprecating joke about it on social media
scenarios.rightOption: Issue a measured statement about commitment to the club

scenarios.scenario: One of your players livestreams himself playing video games at 3am the night before a match. The clip spreads rapidly online.
scenarios.leftOption: Drop him from the squad for the match
scenarios.rightOption: Fine him quietly and let him play — don't make it bigger

scenarios.scenario: A player's partner posts cryptic social media stories suggesting unhappiness at the club. Fans are speculating wildly.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it at your press conference — clarify nothing is wrong
scenarios.rightOption: Let it blow over — engaging only amplifies it

scenarios.scenario: The pitch is waterlogged after heavy rain. Ground staff say it's borderline playable. The match is a crucial six-pointer.
scenarios.leftOption: Request a postponement — safety and quality first
scenarios.rightOption: Play on — conditions are equal for both sides

scenarios.scenario: Your pre-season training trip to a hot climate backfires — three players pick up heat exhaustion and miss the first two league games.
scenarios.leftOption: Take responsibility publicly and focus on recovery
scenarios.rightOption: Blame the medical team for inadequate preparation

scenarios.scenario: Your academy director says a 17-year-old is ready for the first team. Scouts from rival clubs are already watching him at youth level.
scenarios.leftOption: Fast-track him to the first team now
scenarios.rightOption: Tie him to a long contract first before promoting

scenarios.scenario: A youth player's parent calls the club demanding answers on why their son isn't being fast-tracked despite top performances.
scenarios.leftOption: Meet the parents and explain the development plan
scenarios.rightOption: Refer them to the academy director — it's not your call

scenarios.scenario: Your U23 coach resigns unexpectedly citing 'lack of support'. Several academy talents are now unsettled.
scenarios.leftOption: Appoint a first-team coach to temporarily oversee U23s
scenarios.rightOption: Promote a senior academy player to player-coach

scenarios.scenario: A rival manager publicly says your youth development system is 'producing robots, not footballers'. Your academy director is furious.
scenarios.leftOption: Hit back in the press — defend your academy
scenarios.rightOption: Stay above it — let results do the talking

scenarios.scenario: Your top scorer has 6 months left on his contract and hasn't agreed a renewal. January is approaching.
scenarios.leftOption: Sell him in January to get value
scenarios.rightOption: Hold firm — back him to sign when he sees the project

scenarios.scenario: A fringe player whose contract expires this summer asks for a meeting. He wants clarity on his future at the club.
scenarios.leftOption: Offer him a one-year extension — reward his professionalism
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him truthfully he's likely to be released

scenarios.scenario: The board offers your best player an improved deal, but accidentally leaks the wage to squad members. Several players are now demanding parity.
scenarios.leftOption: Level the playing field — offer small raises across the board
scenarios.rightOption: Hold the line — the market rate is what it is

scenarios.scenario: Your first-choice winger reveals privately he's been struggling with anxiety and finds matchday build-up overwhelming.
scenarios.leftOption: Give him space — reduce his minutes and pressure
scenarios.rightOption: Work with the club psychologist and keep him in the team

scenarios.scenario: A player is visibly struggling after a bereavement. The fixture schedule gives no break for the next month.
scenarios.leftOption: Grant him compassionate leave regardless of fixtures
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him involved — routine and teammates can help

scenarios.scenario: After a string of defeats, you notice your players look genuinely flat. The team's body language in training is worrying.
scenarios.leftOption: Cancel training — give everyone two days off
scenarios.rightOption: Run an intense confidence-building session with the psychologist

scenarios.scenario: The chairman calls you in to discuss performances 'privately'. He wants results in the next three games or there will be 'a review'.
scenarios.leftOption: Push back — question why there's no public support
scenarios.rightOption: Accept the pressure — make it your motivation

scenarios.scenario: The board announces a surprise kit deal with a controversial sponsor. Players are uncomfortable. You're asked to endorse it.
scenarios.rightOption: Express reservations publicly — stand with the players

scenarios.scenario: A new investor group is acquiring a stake in the club. They want to meet you and 'align on vision' before the deal closes.
scenarios.leftOption: Meet them — show confidence in the club's direction
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — it's not your place to be involved in ownership deals

scenarios.scenario: The board wants to repurpose the training ground café for corporate hospitality, removing a player relaxation area. Players are annoyed.
scenarios.leftOption: Fight the decision — protect the players' space
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it — pick battles with the board carefully

scenarios.scenario: Three first-team regulars are injured in a single week. You must play youngsters in a high-stakes match.
scenarios.leftOption: Set up defensively — protect the youngsters
scenarios.rightOption: Attack-minded — give them freedom and belief

scenarios.scenario: Your physio believes a key player needs 4 weeks rest. The player insists he can play through it. You're one point off the drop zone.
scenarios.leftOption: Side with the physio — player welfare first
scenarios.rightOption: Let the player decide — it's his body

scenarios.scenario: Your starting XI have all played 90 minutes in the last 4 games. Fatigue stats are alarming but a big match is in 3 days.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest key players and trust the depth
scenarios.rightOption: Play the first XI — momentum is everything right now

scenarios.scenario: Analytics recommend pressing high but your defenders are struggling physically this week. The data conflicts with what you're seeing on the pitch.
scenarios.leftOption: Trust the data — press high regardless
scenarios.rightOption: Trust your eyes — drop into a mid-block

scenarios.scenario: You've played the same system all season but results have dried up. Your senior players are now publicly calling for more variety.
scenarios.leftOption: Shift to a new system — evolution is strength
scenarios.rightOption: Stick rigidly to your philosophy — don't bend to pressure

scenarios.scenario: Your fullbacks are bombing forward constantly. Two goals have been conceded from their positions. Do you rein them in?
scenarios.leftOption: Tell them to stay deeper — shape over flair
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the attacking instinct — accept the defensive risk

scenarios.scenario: The opposing manager accuses you of time-wasting tactics in a post-match interview. The clip has millions of views online.
scenarios.leftOption: Fire back with statistics — your team had more possession
scenarios.rightOption: Rise above — 'I'm focused on my team's development'

scenarios.scenario: A legendary rival manager publicly rates your chances in the title race as 'zero'. His comments dominate the back pages.
scenarios.leftOption: Invite him to watch closely as you prove him wrong
scenarios.rightOption: Agree politely — use it as fuel behind closed doors

scenarios.scenario: After a tight derby loss, you shake hands with the rival manager but he ignores your outstretched hand. Cameras catch it all.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it in the press — call it disrespectful
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it publicly — it says more about him than you

scenarios.scenario: You receive detailed dossiers on two opposing set-piece threats but only have time to prep one before tomorrow's match.
scenarios.leftOption: Focus on their corner taker — he's scored 4 this season
scenarios.rightOption: Focus on their free-kick specialist — he's due one

scenarios.scenario: Your video analyst has prepared two different pre-match films — one motivational, one tactical. You have 20 minutes before kick-off.
scenarios.leftOption: Show the motivational film — fire up their emotions
scenarios.rightOption: Show the tactical film — information beats adrenaline

scenarios.scenario: A player jokingly refers to you as 'The Gaffer with bad taste in music' in a team bonding video shared online. Players love it.
scenarios.leftOption: Lean into the joke — post a playlist publicly
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — you don't do social media personalities

scenarios.scenario: During a rainstorm team huddle, you forget your umbrella and get completely soaked. It becomes the team's running joke for two weeks.
scenarios.leftOption: Embrace it — wear it proudly as a badge of honour
scenarios.rightOption: Move on quickly — you don't like being the punchline

scenarios.scenario: Your star forward scores a hat-trick then credits you personally in the post-match interview, saying 'the gaffer changed my career'. Press goes wild.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept the praise graciously — publicly back him in return
scenarios.rightOption: Deflect to the team — 'it's about the collective'

scenarios.scenario: A child supporter sends you a hand-drawn tactical board with suggestions for the next match. Your assistant frames it and hangs it in the office.
scenarios.leftOption: Reply to the child directly via the club's social media
scenarios.rightOption: Feature it in the matchday programme with a note from you

scenarios.scenario: Your entire coaching staff win the club quiz night against the players. The victory is celebrated with an improvised trophy made from a water bottle.
scenarios.leftOption: Display the trophy in the dugout on matchday
scenarios.rightOption: Give it to the players — good for team relations

scenarios.scenario: You accidentally walk into the wrong press conference room and spend 30 seconds before realising. A reporter films it. It goes viral.
scenarios.leftOption: Own it — send a funny follow-up to the clip
scenarios.rightOption: Let the PR team handle it professionally

scenarios.scenario: Your central midfielder gets his fifth yellow card of the season meaning a one-game ban — for the derby. He's devastated.
scenarios.leftOption: Use the opportunity to start a fresher player
scenarios.rightOption: Appeal the yellow card immediately

scenarios.scenario: A player receives a straight red for violent conduct in training — against a teammate. Cameras were rolling.
scenarios.leftOption: Suspend him immediately and launch a formal investigation
scenarios.rightOption: Handle it internally — a fine and a strong message

scenarios.scenario: Your striker is sent off for a cynical professional foul in the 88th minute of a match you're winning. He's unapologetic in the dressing room.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him and publicly back the red card decision
scenarios.rightOption: Defend him — the foul was tactically justified

scenarios.scenario: Your data team spots an unknown 23-year-old in the second division of Eastern Europe who fits your system perfectly. No scout has seen him live.
scenarios.leftOption: Sign based on data alone — trust the model
scenarios.rightOption: Send a scout in person first — data alone isn't enough

scenarios.scenario: Your head of recruitment wants to bring in expensive proprietary data software. The fee could buy a squad player. The board is neutral.
scenarios.leftOption: Approve the software — long-term efficiency wins
scenarios.rightOption: Pass — invest in the player instead

scenarios.scenario: You're three points clear with three games to go. The squad is visibly nervous. How do you approach the final weeks?
scenarios.leftOption: Keep routines exactly the same — don't change a thing
scenarios.rightOption: Give a rousing speech — acknowledge the pressure directly

scenarios.scenario: You've lost three in a row going into the final five games. The media declares the season over before it's officially done.
scenarios.leftOption: Do a media blackout — shield the players from noise
scenarios.rightOption: Address the criticism head-on — rally with a public statement

scenarios.scenario: The final match of the season is a must-win. Your best player pulls out of the warm-up with a muscle tweak. He wants to play through it.
scenarios.leftOption: Risk it — this season is defined by this match
scenarios.rightOption: Protect him — he's too important to the club's future

scenarios.scenario: A player receives racist abuse during a match. He wants to walk off the pitch. You support him but the referee refuses to stop play.
scenarios.leftOption: Walk your team off in solidarity
scenarios.rightOption: Stay — fight it through official channels after

scenarios.scenario: A player comes out as gay publicly. The club response is warm but some dressing room reactions feel awkward.
scenarios.leftOption: Address the culture directly in a full squad meeting
scenarios.rightOption: Let it normalise naturally — overreacting can also isolate

scenarios.scenario: Two players are from opposing nations currently in a diplomatic dispute. Tension between them is visibly affecting training dynamics.
scenarios.leftOption: Separate them in drills and address it one-on-one
scenarios.rightOption: Force them together — sport transcends politics

scenarios.scenario: The club faces a financial fair play investigation. The board asks you not to sign anyone until it's resolved.
scenarios.rightOption: Push back — you need a striker to survive

scenarios.scenario: A payment dispute between the club and a foreign club over a previous transfer fee lands in the news. Your name is mentioned unfairly.
scenarios.leftOption: Release a personal statement to clear your name
scenarios.rightOption: Let the club's legal team handle it — stay off the record

scenarios.scenario: Your end-of-season player awards ceremony sparks controversy — the captain finishes last in the players' player vote.
scenarios.leftOption: Announce the real winner, business as usual
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly speak to the captain before the results are public

scenarios.scenario: The club wants to arrange a post-season tour to Asia for commercial reasons. Your players desperately need rest.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the tour — revenue supports the club
scenarios.rightOption: Resist — player welfare must come first

scenarios.scenario: A Premier League club wants to loan out a highly-rated midfielder to you for the season. The player's wage demands are above your normal budget.
scenarios.leftOption: Break the wage structure — he's worth it
scenarios.rightOption: Walk away — the deal doesn't make financial sense

scenarios.scenario: A loan player returns to his parent club early citing 'lack of game time'. He played 18 of 22 games. His agent is causing noise.
scenarios.leftOption: Release a statement with his stats — facts over feelings
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go — engaging with agents publicly is a trap

scenarios.scenario: A tech company offers your club AI-powered real-time tactical advice delivered through an earpiece to your assistant during matches. It's legal but unconventional.
scenarios.leftOption: Trial it — gain a technological edge
scenarios.rightOption: Reject it — football is about human instinct

scenarios.scenario: Your fitness team recommends cryotherapy chambers that cost £400k to install. Preliminary evidence shows 20% faster recovery times.
scenarios.leftOption: Approve the investment — recovery is the modern edge
scenarios.rightOption: Invest that money in a player instead

scenarios.scenario: A GPS tracking company's leaked data reveals your training intensity metrics to a rival club scout. The breach is embarrassing.
scenarios.leftOption: Cancel the GPS deal publicly — blame the company
scenarios.rightOption: Quietly renegotiate with stricter data clauses

scenarios.scenario: Your most trusted journalist contact publishes a story clearly sourced from inside your dressing room. It's damaging and specific.
scenarios.leftOption: Cut off your relationship with him entirely
scenarios.rightOption: Call him privately — you need to know who's leaking

scenarios.scenario: A tabloid wants to run an exclusive with your manager profile for a fee. They want editorial control over the questions.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — the visibility is worth it
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — you won't surrender editorial control

scenarios.scenario: Your club is offered naming rights for the stadium by a fast food chain. The deal is huge financially but many supporters are opposed.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the deal — the revenue changes everything
scenarios.rightOption: Side with the fans — reject the naming rights

scenarios.scenario: A documentary crew wants to embed with your team for a month-long behind-the-scenes film. The board loves the idea. Your players are divided.
scenarios.leftOption: Allow it — transparency builds connection with fans
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — the cameras will disrupt preparation

scenarios.scenario: A famous retired footballer with no coaching badges asks to train with your first team for a week as part of a charity initiative.
scenarios.leftOption: Welcome him — the experience could energise the squad
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — it's a distraction from serious preparation

scenarios.scenario: Your groundskeeper tells you the pitch quality is terrible and will affect your passing game significantly against a physical opponent.
scenarios.leftOption: Water it heavily the night before to slow opponents down
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it — playing conditions must be fair

scenarios.scenario: You discover a physio has been sharing injury updates with a rival club's staff for personal favours.
scenarios.leftOption: Sack the physio immediately and report to the authorities
scenarios.rightOption: Give a final warning and implement stricter protocols

scenarios.scenario: Your striker has scored in 7 straight games. A hamstring niggle means the medical team recommends resting him for the next match.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest him — protect the golden run long-term
scenarios.rightOption: Start him — the streak is momentum you can't break

scenarios.scenario: The referee confirms after a match that a goal that was ruled out should have stood. The result cost you three points.
scenarios.leftOption: Launch a formal complaint to the FA
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it and move on — complaining never wins points

scenarios.scenario: A transfer window rumour links you to a move away from the club. It's completely false but the board wants a public denial.
scenarios.leftOption: Issue a strong public statement of commitment
scenarios.rightOption: Let it pass — dignifying it gives it oxygen

scenarios.scenario: Your club's owner dies unexpectedly. The club releases a statement but the squad is visibly shaken. A match is scheduled in two days.
scenarios.leftOption: Request a postponement out of respect
scenarios.rightOption: Play — honour him with a result on the pitch

scenarios.scenario: A player who refused to be substituted last week publicly apologises in the media without consulting you first.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept it graciously and move on
scenarios.rightOption: Address it directly — the apology should have come to you first

scenarios.scenario: Your goalkeeper has just conceded 3 in a row from long shots. He's clearly struggling with distance perception. The physio suggests a vision test.
scenarios.rightOption: Build his confidence first — tests can wait

scenarios.scenario: The club mascot goes viral for the wrong reasons — captured on camera berating a young fan. You're asked to respond.
scenarios.leftOption: Retire the mascot immediately and apologise
scenarios.rightOption: Investigate first before making a public response

scenarios.scenario: A referee calls 5 fouls on your striker in a match, none of which look physical. Your striker is frustrated and you see a 6th challenge coming.
scenarios.leftOption: Instruct him to ease off physical play completely
scenarios.rightOption: Tell him to keep playing his game — the referee is wrong

scenarios.scenario: Your defender posts a coded social media message after being dropped that fans read as a transfer request. He denies it.
scenarios.leftOption: Call him in and demand clarity
scenarios.rightOption: Take his denial at face value publicly

scenarios.scenario: Two of your players are seen arguing in a car park outside training. A fan films it. Neither will explain what happened.
scenarios.leftOption: Separate them in training groups while you investigate
scenarios.rightOption: Lock them in a room together and tell them to sort it

scenarios.scenario: Your captain loses his cool at a teammate in the pre-match warm-up, visibly in front of the travelling fans.
scenarios.leftOption: Sub the captain out of the starting XI — can't start fired up like that
scenarios.rightOption: Channel it — that's leadership energy, keep him on

scenarios.scenario: Your training sessions are rated 'most intense in the league' by a sports science report. Three players are picking up minor fatigue injuries.
scenarios.leftOption: Reduce intensity — load management is modern best practice
scenarios.rightOption: Maintain it — fitness levels are your competitive edge

scenarios.scenario: A first team coach wants to introduce a silent training drill policy — no verbal communication allowed. Players think it's bizarre.
scenarios.leftOption: Try it — new methods build new thinking
scenarios.rightOption: Veto it — communication is football's foundation

scenarios.scenario: An academic researcher asks to publish a study on your training methodologies. Some of your tactical data would be included.
scenarios.leftOption: Permit it — sharing knowledge elevates the game
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — competitive advantage stays in-house

scenarios.scenario: A first-team player publicly praises a rival manager's style of play in an interview, drawing unflattering comparisons to yours.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it directly in training — that's disloyal
scenarios.rightOption: Ignore it — players can have opinions

scenarios.scenario: Your set-piece routines are leaked by a journalist the night before a crucial match. You suspect it came from within.
scenarios.leftOption: Change the entire set-piece playbook overnight
scenarios.rightOption: Keep the original plan — don't let paranoia disrupt preparation

scenarios.scenario: Your new signing's family hasn't settled in the city. He's clearly distracted and performances are well below expected levels.
scenarios.leftOption: Give the family a relocation support package — invest in his happiness
scenarios.rightOption: Give him an ultimatum — perform or the experiment ends

scenarios.scenario: The charity match you committed to coincides with a sudden fixture rescheduling. You'll need to send a weakened side to one of them.
scenarios.leftOption: Honour the charity match — send first team there
scenarios.rightOption: Prioritise the league fixture — send fringe players to charity

scenarios.scenario: A youth player refuses to wear the new sponsor's logo on his boots for religious reasons. It's within rights but the sponsor complains.
scenarios.leftOption: Back the player — religious freedom is non-negotiable
scenarios.rightOption: Negotiate a compromise with the sponsor quietly

scenarios.scenario: The media claims a former player you sold is outperforming your entire current forward line. The comparison dominates the sports pages.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it head-on — call it unfair context
scenarios.rightOption: Silence speaks louder — let the squad respond with results

scenarios.scenario: You are offered the chance to co-author a book on football tactics with a former world-class manager. It would take evenings for three months.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — the intellectual challenge and profile boost are worth it
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — your sole focus must be the club right now

scenarios.scenario: The TV channel holding rights to your next home match requests you wear a mic during the game for a special broadcast feature.
scenarios.rightOption: Refuse — your touchline communication is private

scenarios.scenario: During a training drill, you accidentally kick a ball that hits the physio in the face. The clip spreads internally. Players are in hysterics.
scenarios.leftOption: Laugh it off — lean into the moment with the squad
scenarios.rightOption: Apologise sincerely and move the session on quickly

scenarios.scenario: You are awarded manager of the month. One of your senior players publicly says 'the players deserve it more than the manager'.
scenarios.leftOption: Share the award and the credit publicly with the squad
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go — he's entitled to his opinion

scenarios.scenario: A foreign player in your squad is struggling with English. Teammates are finding it hard to connect with him on and off the pitch.
scenarios.leftOption: Hire a language coach and pair him with a buddy player
scenarios.rightOption: Trust the natural integration process — it takes time

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant manager has been secretly applying for other manager jobs. You find out from a third party and he doesn't know you know.
scenarios.leftOption: Confront him directly — you need loyalty
scenarios.rightOption: Let it go — ambition is natural, focus on results

scenarios.scenario: Your club's social media team asks to create a TikTok series featuring you in behind-the-scenes clips. The board thinks it's brilliant.
scenarios.leftOption: Participate — modern clubs need modern managers
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — keep the mystique and authority

scenarios.scenario: A data analyst flags that your opponent's goalkeeper struggles under high balls in training clips. It's not been tested in a real game.
scenarios.leftOption: Build a game plan around it — exploit the potential weakness
scenarios.rightOption: Don't rely on untested intel — could be a trap

scenarios.scenario: Your team bus is stuck in traffic 45 minutes before kick-off due to a protest march. You're cutting it dangerously close.
scenarios.leftOption: Have players warm up on the bus — adapt
scenarios.rightOption: Request a 15-minute kick-off delay from officials

scenarios.scenario: The opposition manager calls your 'parking the bus' tactics cynical and cowardly in a press conference. Fans of both clubs are now inflamed.
scenarios.leftOption: Defend pragmatism — winning ugly is still winning
scenarios.rightOption: Challenge him to a tactical debate publicly

scenarios.scenario: One of your backroom staff members wins a large sum on a lottery. He announces he's leaving football entirely. He was crucial to team coordination.
scenarios.leftOption: Wish him well and promote from within the club
scenarios.rightOption: Offer a massive retention bonus to keep him

scenarios.scenario: Your match analyst has built a predictive model that correctly called your last 3 opponents' formations. He wants to brief the board on AI-driven scouting.
scenarios.leftOption: Support it fully — innovation needs visibility
scenarios.rightOption: Keep it internal — the less rivals know, the better

scenarios.scenario: An ex-player you released two years ago has become a fan favourite at a rival club and scores against you. The crowd taunts you.
scenarios.leftOption: Acknowledge it with class in the post-match presser
scenarios.rightOption: Dismiss it — selection decisions are never personal

scenarios.scenario: Your kit man retires after 20 years of service. He wants a guard of honour from the players on his last day.
scenarios.leftOption: Organise it — culture matters more than training time
scenarios.rightOption: Organise a quiet farewell separately — don't disrupt the schedule

scenarios.scenario: After a training brawl breaks out between two senior players, both come to you separately claiming the other started it.
scenarios.leftOption: Suspend both for one game each — no exceptions
scenarios.rightOption: Watch the training footage yourself before deciding

scenarios.scenario: Your club's shirt number 9 is considered cursed — five consecutive players have had injury-hit seasons wearing it. A new signing wants it.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him have it — superstitions have no place in football
scenarios.rightOption: Suggest a different number — morale is about belief

scenarios.scenario: Your centre-back has been called up for international duty during a crucial league run. You can legally block the call-up on medical grounds.
scenarios.leftOption: Block it — the club's survival matters more
scenarios.rightOption: Release him — international football is his right

scenarios.scenario: The club's catering team accidentally serves the squad undercooked chicken the day before a match. Three players feel unwell.
scenarios.leftOption: Rest all three — health is non-negotiable
scenarios.rightOption: Leave it to the players to decide — they know their own bodies

scenarios.scenario: A sports betting company wants to do a podcast collaboration with you discussing match predictions. They offer a significant fee.
scenarios.leftOption: Decline — conflict of interest around match discussions
scenarios.rightOption: Accept — as long as no internal information is shared

scenarios.scenario: You spot a promising trialist in a reserve game. He's a free agent but three bigger clubs have also noticed him this week.
scenarios.leftOption: Sign him immediately on a short-term deal to block others
scenarios.rightOption: Monitor him one more game before committing

scenarios.scenario: A supporter who runs a popular fan channel publicly feuds with your official social media team. Fans are split on who's right.
scenarios.leftOption: Invite the fan channel creator for a private chat to resolve it
scenarios.rightOption: Support your club staff publicly — internal solidarity first

scenarios.scenario: You catch a board member watching your private team meeting through a glass window. He claims he was just passing by.
scenarios.leftOption: Call it out directly — boundaries need to be reset
scenarios.rightOption: Mention it casually to the chairman — no confrontation

scenarios.scenario: Your club is invited to participate in a new winter tournament with significant prize money but it requires three extra matches in an already packed December.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — the prize money and exposure are valuable
scenarios.rightOption: Decline — squad welfare over commercial interest in December

scenarios.scenario: You are challenged to a public five-a-side match against a celebrity team raising money for charity. It would dominate social media.
scenarios.leftOption: Accept — participate fully and play to win
scenarios.rightOption: Send a squad of youth players in your place

scenarios.scenario: Your number one goalkeeper requests a move to first-choice status at another club. Your backup is untested but technically impressive.
scenarios.leftOption: Let him go and back the backup to grow
scenarios.rightOption: Keep him — proven quality beats untested potential

scenarios.scenario: Your club's DJ at home matches plays a song the visiting fans find offensive. It causes a near-incident in the stands.
scenarios.rightOption: Defend it — home fans chose it, it's tradition

scenarios.scenario: During a post-match debrief you discover one player had his earbuds in listening to music during your tactical talk. He scored twice in the match.
scenarios.leftOption: Make an example of the rule breach publicly in the group
scenarios.rightOption: Laugh it off privately — the result speaks for itself

scenarios.scenario: Your starting goalkeeper accidentally breaks a fridge in the dressing room in celebration. Three players catch it on camera.
scenarios.leftOption: Fine him the cost of the fridge as a lighthearted gesture
scenarios.rightOption: Celebrate the energy — it shows passion

scenarios.scenario: Your assistant accidentally sends a voice note meant for you — criticising a senior player — to the full squad group chat.
scenarios.leftOption: Address it openly with the squad immediately
scenarios.rightOption: Delete it quietly and speak to the player privately

scenarios.scenario: Three academy players are discovered to have been betting small amounts on their own team's matches — technically a breach of rules but completely naive.
scenarios.leftOption: Report it formally — rules are rules regardless of intent
scenarios.rightOption: Handle internally with education and a warning

scenarios.scenario: Your new signing's first touch in a competitive match is met with boos from a section of your own supporters who wanted a different transfer.
scenarios.leftOption: Publicly back the signing in the post-match presser
scenarios.rightOption: Speak to the player privately — protect him from the noise

scenarios.scenario: Your club's CEO announces plans for a new training ground without consulting you on the design. The layout doesn't suit your training philosophy.
scenarios.leftOption: Request urgent changes before ground is broken
scenarios.rightOption: Accept it — facilities funding is rare, don't bite the hand
